blocks:
Sūrat al-Anbiyāʾ

Meccan, and it is 112 verses.

Sūrat al-Anbiyāʾ

It is entirely Meccan, and it consists of one hundred and twelve verses according to the Kufan count, and one hundred and eleven verses according to the others. The difference lies in one verse: ما لا يَنْفَعُكُمْ شَيْئاً وَلا يَضُرُّكُمْ ** (Kufan). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) اقْتَرَبَ لِلنَّاسِ حِسابُهُمْ (1) (The people’s reckoning has drawn near) In al-Qummi: The Resurrection (القيامة), the Hour (السّاعة), and the Reckoning (الحساب) have drawn near. And in al-Majmaʿ: It was only described as near because one of the signs (أشراط) of the Hour is the sending of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for He said: "I and the Hour were sent like these two," (and He pointed with His index and middle fingers). And in al-Jawamiʿ, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "Indeed, the world has turned away swiftly (حذّاء), and nothing remains of it except a remnant (صُبابة), like the remnant in a vessel." وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ مُعْرِضُونَ ** (while they are oblivious, turning away.) [They are] in oblivion (غفلة) regarding the Reckoning, turning away (معرضون) from reflecting upon it.

(٢) ما يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ ذِكْرٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ ** (2) There does not come to them any fresh reminder from their Lord [It] alerts them from the path of heedlessness (الغفلة) and ignorance (الجهالة) مُحْدَثٍ ** so that the warning may be repeated upon their hearing, in order that they may take heed إِلَّا اسْتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ ** they mock, they ridicule it, due to the extremity of their heedlessness and the excess of their turning away from reflecting upon matters (الأمور) and contemplating the consequences (العواقب). لاهِيَةً قُلُوبُهُمْ**

(٣) القمّيّ قال من التلهي وَأَسَرُّوا النَّجْوَى (And they concealed the secret counsel): They exaggerated in hiding it, or they made it such that their secret conversation (تناجيهم) was hidden by it. الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (Those who did wrong) is an appositional substitute (بدل) for the waw (the subject pronoun) in أسرُّوا (they concealed), indicating that they are wrongdoers (ظالمون) in what they concealed. هَلْ هذا إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ أَفَتَأْتُونَ السِّحْرَ وَأَنْتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ ** (Is this but a human being like yourselves? Will you then yield to magic while you see?) It was said that it is as if they used the fact that He is a human being as proof of His falsehood in claiming the Messengership (الرسالة), due to their belief that the Messenger can only be an angel (ملكاً). And they necessarily inferred from this that what He brought of extraordinary things (الخوارق), such as the Quran, is magic. So they denied His presence (or the truth of His message). And they only concealed it for consultation (تشاوراً) regarding the derivation (استنباط) of what would destroy His affair and reveal its corruption to the people generally (عامّة).

(٤) قالَ رَبِّي يَعْلَمُ الْقَوْلَ فِي السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (4) He said, 'My Lord knows every word [said] in the heaven and the earth) whether it was manifest (جهراً) or secret (سرّاً). And قال (He said) was read (as a statement) reporting from the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ ** (and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.') So, what they conceal (يسرّون) and what they hide in their hearts (يضمرون) is not hidden from Him. (٥) بَلْ قالُوا أَضْغاثُ أَحْلامٍ بَلِ افْتَراهُ بَلْ هُوَ شاعِرٌ ** (5) Rather they say, '[They are] confused dreams,' rather, 'He has fabricated it,' rather, 'He is a poet!) This is a retraction (اضراب) by them, shifting from their saying: "It is magic (سحر)" to [saying] that it is confused dreams (تخاليط الأحلام), then to [saying] that it is speech he fabricated, and then to [saying] that it is the saying of a poet. فَلْيَأْتِنا بِآيَةٍ كَما أُرْسِلَ ** (Let him bring us a sign, just as the [apostles] were sent) with it الْأَوَّلُونَ ** (before him.') such as the White Hand (اليد البيضاء), the staff (العصا), the healing of the born-blind (إبراء الأكمه), and the resurrection of the dead (إحياء الموتى).

(٦) ما آمَنَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ ** meaning: from the people of a township (من أهل قرية) أَهْلَكْناها ** due to their demanding the signs (الآيات) when they came to them. أَفَهُمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** while they (the current disbelievers) are more rebellious (أعتى) than them (the previous nations). Al-Qummiyy said: How can they believe when those who were before them did not believe in the signs (الآيات) until they perished?

ما آمَنَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْناها أَفَهُمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٦) (6) Not one of the townships that We destroyed before them believed. Will they then believe?

(7) وَما أَرْسَلْنا قَبْلَكَ إِلَّا رِجالاً نُوحِي إِلَيْهِمْ ** (We did not send before you, [O Muhammad], except men to whom We revealed) And nūḥī (نُوحي) was recited with the nūn (ن). فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** (So ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know.) It is said that this is a response to their saying: هَلْ هذا إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ. (Is this but a human like yourselves?)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "Indeed, those among us claim that the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ (So ask the People of the Reminder), refers to the Jews and the Christians." He said: "Then He would be calling you to their religion." Then He said, and He pointed with His hand toward His chest: "We are the People of the Reminder (أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ), and we are the ones who are questioned (المسئولون)." This narration, along with other reports concerning this meaning, has already preceded in Surat al-Naḥl with clarification (بيان).

(٨) وَما جَعَلْناهُمْ جَسَداً لا يَأْكُلُونَ الطَّعامَ وَما كانُوا خالِدِينَ ** (8) We did not make them bodies that did not eat food, nor were they immortal. This is a negation of what they believed: that prophethood (الرسالة) is exclusive to the angels (خواصّ الملك). (٩) ثُمَّ صَدَقْناهُمُ الْوَعْدَ ** (9) Then We fulfilled Our promise to them, meaning, in the promise. فَأَنْجَيْناهُمْ وَمَنْ نَشاءُ ** and delivered them and whomever We wished, meaning, those who believed in them, and those in whose preservation there was wisdom, such as one who would believe, either himself or one of his descendants (ذرّيته). وَأَهْلَكْنَا الْمُسْرِفِينَ ** and We destroyed the extravagant. in disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي).

(١٠) لَقَدْ أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكُمْ ** (We have certainly sent down to you) O Quraysh (يا قريش) كِتاباً ** (a Book) meaning the Quran (يعني القرآن) فِيهِ ذِكْرُكُمْ ** (in which is your mention) meaning your fame (صيتكم) or your admonition (موعظتكم) أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ ** (Will you not then apply reason?) so that you may believe (فتؤمنون). (We have certainly sent down to you a Book in which is your mention. Will you not then apply reason?)

(١١) وَكَمْ قَصَمْنا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ كانَتْ ظالِمَةً وَأَنْشَأْنا بَعْدَها ** (How many a town We have crushed that was iniquitous, and raised up after it) after the destruction (إهلاك) of its people, a people (قَوْماً) آخَرِينَ ** (another people!) in their place (مكانهم). (How many a town We have crushed that was iniquitous, and raised up after it another people!)

(١٢) فَلَمَّا أَحَسُّوا بَأْسَنا ** (When they sensed Our might) meaning, when they perceived the severity of Our punishment (شدّة عذابنا) with the perception (ادراك) of something witnessed and felt (المشاهد المحسوس) إِذا هُمْ مِنْها يَرْكُضُونَ ** (behold, they started fleeing from it) they flee (يهربون) quickly (مسرعين). (When they sensed Our might, behold, they started fleeing from it.)

(١٣) لا تَرْكُضُوا ** (Do not flee) this is based on the intention of speech (على إرادة القول), meaning it was said to them mockingly (قيل لهم استهزاء) وَارْجِعُوا إِلى ما أُتْرِفْتُمْ فِيهِ ** (but return to the luxury you were given) meaning the enjoyment (التنعّم) and pleasure (التلذّذ). And الإتراف is the rejection of grace (ابطار النّعمة) وَمَساكِنِكُمْ ** (and your dwellings) which belonged to you (الّتي كانت لكم) لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْئَلُونَ ** (so that you may be questioned.) (Do not flee, but return to the luxury you were given and your dwellings, so that you may be questioned.)

(١٤) قالُوا يا وَيْلَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ (14) They said, 'Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!' (١٥) فَما زالَتْ تِلْكَ دَعْواهُمْ ** (15) That continued to be their cry They did not cease repeating that. It was only called a cry (دَعْواهُمْ) because the one who wails (المولول) is as if he is calling upon woe (الويل), saying: "O Woe, come! This is your time." حَتَّى جَعَلْناهُمْ حَصِيداً ** until We turned them into a mown field, extinguished. And حَصِيداً (mown field) is the harvested plant, خامِدِينَ (extinguished) meaning dead, derived from when the fire is extinguished (خمدت النار). It is said that this was revealed concerning the people of Yemen who denied their Prophet, Hanzalah (حنظلة), and killed him. So, Allah empowered Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصّر) over them until he destroyed them by the sword. And the meaning of لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْئَلُونَ (that you may be questioned) is that you may be questioned about something from your worldly possessions, for you are people of wealth and blessing (نعمة). And this is a mockery of them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Allah has certainly made you hear in His Book what He did to the wrongdoing people of the towns before you, where He said: وَكَمْ قَصَمْنا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ كانَتْ ظالِمَةً ** (How many a town have We crushed while it was wrongdoing) And by the town (القرية), He only meant its inhabitants, where He says: وَأَنْشَأْنا بَعْدَها قَوْماً آخَرِينَ ** (and raised up after it another people.) Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَلَمَّا أَحَسُّوا بَأْسَنا إِذا هُمْ مِنْها يَرْكُضُونَ ** (But when they sensed Our might, behold, they started fleeing from it.) Meaning: they flee (يهربون). He said: "So when the punishment came to them, they said: يا وَيْلَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ** (Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!)." He said: "And by Allah, this is an admonition for you and a warning, if you take heed and fear."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When the Riser (القائم) rises and sends [forces] to Banu Umayyah in Syria (الشام), they will flee to the Romans (الرّوم). The Romans will say to them: "We will not admit you until you become Christians (تتنصروا)." So, they will hang crosses (الصّلبان) around their necks, and [the Romans] will admit them. When the companions of the Riser (peace be upon him) descend upon their presence, they will seek safety (الأمان) and reconciliation (الصلح). The companions of the Riser (peace be upon him) will say: "We will not do so until you hand over to us those among you who belong to us." So, they will hand them over to them. That is His saying: لا تَرْكَنُوا (Do not incline) up to His saying: لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْئَلُونَ ** (that you may be questioned) He said: He will question them about the treasures (الكنوز), and He is more knowledgeable of them. He said: Then they will say: يا وَيْلَنا ** (Woe to us!) up to His saying: خامِدِينَ ** (extinguished) Meaning: by the sword. And that is Sa'id ibn 'Abd al-Malik al-Umawi (سعيد بن عبد الملك الامويّ), the owner of the Sa'id River (نهر سعيد) in al-Ruhbah (بالرّحبة). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated something close to this. He said: All of this is among that whose wording is past tense (ماض) but whose meaning is future tense (مستقبل), and it is among what We mentioned whose interpretation (تأويله) comes after its revelation (تنزيله).

(١٦) **وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماءَ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما لاعِبِينَ ** (We did not create the heaven and the earth and whatever is between them in play.)

Rather, We only created the two of them as an insight for those who contemplate and as a reminder for those possessing reflection, and as a means by which the affairs of the servants are regulated in this life (المعاش) and the Hereafter (المعاد). So it is necessary that they should attain through them the achievement of perfection (الكمال), and they should not be deceived by its swiftly perishing adornments.

(١٧) لَوْ أَرَدْنا أَنْ نَتَّخِذَ لَهْواً ** (17) Had We intended to take a diversion, that by which one diverts himself and plays, لَاتَّخَذْناهُ مِنْ لَدُنَّا ** We would have surely taken it from Ourselves, It was said: meaning, from the direction of Our power (قدرتنا), or from Us, from that which is befitting to Our Presence (حَضْرَتُنَا)—from the spiritual beings (الرّوحانيات), not from the physical bodies (الأجسام). **إِنْ كُنَّا فاعِلِينَ ** if We were to do that. that.

(18) بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْباطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ ** (18) But We hurl the truth against the falsehood, and it smashes it meaning: So He obliterates it (فيمحقه). فَإِذا هُوَ زاهِقٌ ** and behold, it vanishes! meaning: Perishing (هالك). This is a transition (إضراب) away from the adoption of amusement (اللهو) and a declaration of the transcendence (تنزيه) of His essence (glory be to Him) from play (اللّعب). Meaning: It is Our way (من شأننا) to make the Truth (الحقّ), which includes seriousness (الجدّ), prevail over falsehood (الباطل), which includes amusement (اللهو). The term al-qadhf (القذف), which is the distant hurling (الرّمي البعيد) implying the hardness (صلابة) of what is hurled, and al-damgh (الدمغ), which is the smashing of the brain (كسر الدّماغ) such that its covering (غثاؤه) is split, leading to the perishing of the spirit (زهوق الرّوح), are borrowed metaphors (استعير) to illustrate the nullification of falsehood by the Truth and to emphasize this nullification (مبالغة فيه). وَلَكُمُ الْوَيْلُ مِمَّا تَصْفُونَ ** Woe to you for what you allege!** Because of what you attribute to Him which is not permissible for Him.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no falsehood (باطل) that stands against the Truth (حقّ) except that the Truth overcomes the falsehood. And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَى الْباطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُ فَإِذا هُوَ زاهِقٌ (18) But We hurl the truth against the falsehood, and it smashes it, and behold, it vanishes!

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no one upon whom the Truth (الحقّ) is presented except that it splits his heart (يصدع قلبه) until he accepts it or abandons it. That is because Allah says in His Book: بَلْ نَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِ (But We hurl the truth) the Ayah [to the end].

(١٩) وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth) as creation and dominion وَمَنْ عِنْدَهُ ** (and those who are near Him) meaning the angels لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبادَتِهِ وَلا يَسْتَحْسِرُونَ ** (do not disdain to worship Him, nor do they grow weary.) and they do not become exhausted from it. (٢٠) يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ ** (They glorify [Him] night and day;) they purify Him and magnify Him constantly لا يَفْتُرُونَ. (they do not flag.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Indeed, the angels are infallible (معصومون), protected from disbelief (الكفر) and vile deeds (القبائح) by the graces (ألطاف) of Allah the Almighty. Allah said concerning them: لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ ** (They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they carry out what they are commanded.) And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ عِنْدَهُ ** (And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth and those who are near Him) meaning the angels لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** (do not disdain)... the Ayah (الآية).

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the angels, "Do they sleep?" He replied: "There is no living being (حيّ) except that it sleeps, save Allah alone. And the angels sleep." It was said: "But Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ لا يَفْتُرُونَ (They glorify [Him] night and day; they do not flag)." He said: "Their breaths (أنفاسهم) are glorification (تسبيح)."

And in a narration (رواية): There is nothing from the layers (أطباق) of their bodies except that it glorifies Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and praises Him from its side with different voices (أصوات مختلفة).

footnote (١) اللهو : المرأة وقيل هو الولد. (١) Al-Lahw (اللهو): The woman, and it is said it is the child (الولد).

(٢١) أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا آلِهَةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ هُمْ يُنْشِرُونَ ** (Or have they taken gods from the earth who raise the dead?) Rather, they have taken (them). And the hamzah (interrogative particle) is for denying their taking (of gods). هُمْ يُنْشِرُونَ ** (meaning) the dead (الموتى). And they, even if they did not explicitly state it, yet their claim of divinity (الإلهيّة) for them (the gods) necessitates it, for among its necessities is the power (الاقتدار) over that (raising the dead). And what is intended by it is to declare them ignorant (تجهيلهم) and to mock them (التهكّم بهم).

(٢٢) لَوْ كانَ فِيهِما آلِهَةٌ إِلَّا اللهُ ** (If there had been in them gods other than Allah,) other than Allah لَفَسَدَتا ** (they would certainly have fallen into ruin.) they would have been nullified and split apart. And indeed, soundness (الصّلاح) has been found, which is the permanence and existence of the universe. So this indicates that the One Who brought it into existence is one, and He is Allah, exalted be His Majesty (جلّ جلاله).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He was asked: "What is the proof (الدّليل) that Allah is One?" He said: "The coherence (اتّصال) of the administration (التدبير) and the perfection (كمال) of the creation (الصنع)," just as the Almighty said: لَوْ كانَ فِيهِما آلِهَةٌ إِلَّا اللهُ لَفَسَدَتا (If there had been in them gods other than Allah, they would certainly have fallen into ruin.)

فَسُبْحانَ اللهِ رَبِّ الْعَرْشِ ** (So immaculate is Allah, the Lord of the Throne,) the One Who encompasses all bodies (الأجسام), which is the locus (محلّ) of administrations (التدابير) and the source (منشأ) of decrees (المقادير), عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ ** (above what they attribute [to Him].) such as taking a partner (الشريك), a consort (الصاحبة), or a child (الولد).

(٢٣) لا يُسْئَلُ عَمَّا يَفْعَلُ (He will not be questioned concerning what He does) due to His greatness, the strength of His dominion, and His uniqueness in divinity (الالوهيّة) and inherent sovereignty (السلطنة الذاتية). وَهُمْ يُسْئَلُونَ (but they will be questioned) because they are owned (مملوكون) and enslaved (مستعبدون).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He means by that His creation, that they will be questioned.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked, "And how is it that لا يُسْئَلُ عَمَّا يَفْعَلُ (He will not be questioned concerning what He does)?" He replied: "Because He does not do anything except what is wisdom (حكمة) and right (صواباً). He is the Proud (المتكبّر), the Compeller (الجبّار), the One (الواحد), the All-dominant (القهّار). So, whoever finds in his soul any constraint (حرجاً) regarding anything He has decreed has committed disbelief (كفر), and whoever denies anything of His actions has rejected [the truth] (جحد)."

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: Allah the Almighty said: "O son of Adam, by My Will you existed. You are the one who wills for yourself what you will, and by My power you performed your obligatory duties (فرائضي) to Me, and by My favor you gained strength for disobeying Me. I made you hearing, seeing, and strong. ما أَصابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللهِ وَما أَصابَكَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِنْ نَفْسِكَ (Whatever good befalls you is from Allah, and whatever evil befalls you is from yourself.) And that is because I am more deserving of your good deeds (حَسناتِكَ) than you are, and you are more deserving of your evil deeds (سيّئاتِك) than I am. And that is because I will not be questioned concerning what I do, but they will be questioned."

(٢٤) أَمِ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ آلِهَةً ** (24) Have they taken gods besides Him? He repeated it (the question) out of magnification of their disbelief (كفرهم), abhorrence of their affair (أمرهم), refutation (تبكيتاً), and demonstration of their effort (جهدهم). قُلْ هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ ** Say, 'Produce your proof.' for that (claim), for the saying of that for which there is no proof is not valid. هذا ذِكْرُ مَنْ مَعِيَ وَذِكْرُ مَنْ قَبْلِي ** This is the Reminder of those who are with me, and the Reminder of those who were before me. It was said: meaning, from the heavenly Books (الكتب السماوية). Look, and will you find in them anything except the command for Monotheism (التّوحيد) and the prohibition of association (of partners, الاشراك)? In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by ذِكْرُ مَنْ مَعِيَ ** (the Reminder of those who are with me) what is to come (ما هو كائن), and by ذِكْرُ مَنْ قَبْلِي ** (the Reminder of those who were before me) what has already been (ما قد كان). بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ الْحَقَ ** Rather, most of them do not know the truth, nor do they distinguish between it (the truth) and falsehood (الباطل). فَهُمْ مُعْرِضُونَ ** so they turn away. from Monotheism (التّوحيد) and following the Messenger (الرّسول) because of that.

(٢٥) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا نُوحِي إِلَيْهِ ** (25) We did not send any apostle before you but We revealed to him And it was read with the nūn (نُوحِي إِلَيْهِ) [followed by] أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاعْبُدُونِ (that there is no god except Me, so worship Me), [which serves as] emphasis (تأكيد) and generalization (تعميم). أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاعْبُدُونِ (25) that there is no god except Me, so worship Me.**

(٢٦) وَقالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمنُ وَلَداً ** (26) And they say, 'The All-beneficent has taken a son.' It is said that it was revealed concerning Khuzāʿah, when they said that the angels are the daughters of Allah. And al-Qummi said: It is what the Christians said that the Messiah is the son of Allah, and what the Jews said: ʿUzayr is the son of Allah. And they said concerning the Imams (peace be upon them) what they said. So Allah, glory be to Him, said: Glory be to Him! (26) out of disdain (أنفة) for Him. Rather, they are honoured servants (26) meaning these [beings] whom they claimed were the children of Allah. He said: And the response to these [claims] is in Sūrat al-Zumar, in His saying: لَوْ أَرادَ اللهُ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ وَلَداً لَاصْطَفى مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ سُبْحانَهُ. (4) Had Allah intended to take a son, He would have chosen whatever He wished from what He creates. Glory be to Him!**

(27) لا يَسْبِقُونَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ ** (27) They do not precede Him in speech, They do not say anything until He says it, just as is the characteristic (شيمة) of well-mannered slaves (العبيد المؤدّبين). وَهُمْ بِأَمْرِهِ يَعْمَلُونَ ** and they act by His command. They never perform an action that He has not commanded them to do. In al-Khara'ij, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): A man and a woman disputed before him, and the man's voice rose above the woman's. So Ali (peace be upon him) said to him: "Be gone (اخسأ)!" He was a Khariji (خارجيّاً). Suddenly, his head became the head of a dog. A man said to him: "O Commander of the Faithful, you shouted at this Khariji (الخارجي) and his head became the head of a dog. What prevents you from [doing the same to] Mu'awiyah?" He replied: "Woe unto you! If I wished to bring Mu'awiyah here with his throne, I would pray to Allah until He did it. However, Allah has treasurers (خزّان) who are not [in charge] of gold or silver, but rather of secrets (اسرار)." This is the interpretation (تأويل) of what you recite: **بَلْ عِبادٌ مُكْرَمُونَ ** (the rest of) the Ayah.

(٢٨) يَعْلَمُ ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَما خَلْفَهُمْ ** (28) He knows what is before them and what is behind them, And nothing is hidden from Him of what they have put forth and what they have left behind. And this is like the reason (العلّة) for what preceded it and the preparation (التمهيد) for what follows it, for they, due to their encompassing knowledge of that, control themselves and monitor their states. وَلا يَشْفَعُونَ إِلَّا لِمَنِ ارْتَضى**. (28) and they do not intercede except for him whom He approves.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): "Except for him whom He approves" means: Allah approves his religion (دينه).

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): And the perpetrators of prescribed punishments (أصحاب الحدود) are transgressors (فسّاق), neither believers (مؤمنون) nor disbelievers (كافرون). They will not abide eternally in the Fire, and they will exit it one day. Intercession (الشفاعة) is permissible for them and for the oppressed (للمستضعفين) if Allah approves their religion (دينهم).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Kazim, from his father, from his forefathers, from the Messenger of Allah (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and them), who said: "Indeed, My intercession (شفاعتي) is only for the perpetrators of major sins (أهل الكبائر) among My nation (أمّتي). As for the virtuous among them (المحسنون), there is no way against them." It was said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, how can intercession be for the perpetrators of major sins when Allah the Almighty says: And they do not intercede except for him whom He approves, and whoever commits a major sin is not approved (مرتضى)?" He said: "There is no believer who commits a sin except that it grieves him and he regrets it."

And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Regret (النّدم) suffices as repentance (توبة)." And He said: "Whoever is pleased by a good deed (حسنة) and grieved by a bad deed (سيّئة) is a believer (مؤمن)." So, whoever does not regret a sin he commits is not a believer, and intercession is not obligatory for him, and he is an oppressor (ظالماً). And Allah, may His mention be exalted, says: ما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَلا شَفِيعٍ يُطاعُ ** (18) The wrongdoers will have no friend nor any intercessor who will be heard. So it was said to Him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, how is it that one who does not regret a sin he commits is not a believer?" He said: "There is no one who commits a major sin (كبيرة) while knowing that He will punish him for it, except that he regrets what he committed. And whenever he regrets, he is repentant (تائباً) and deserving of intercession. And whenever he does not regret it, he is persistent (مصرّاً). And the persistent one (المصرّ) is not forgiven, because he is not a believer in the punishment (عقوبة) for what he committed. If he were a believer in the punishment, he would have regretted it. And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: 'There is no major sin (كبيرة) with seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار), and no minor sin (صغيرة) with persistence (الإصرار).'" As for the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: And they do not intercede except for him whom He approves, they do not intercede except for him whose religion (دينه) Allah approves. And the religion (الدين) is the affirmation (الإقرار) of recompense (الجزاء) for good deeds (الحسنات) and bad deeds (السيئات). So, whoever's religion He approves, regrets the sins he committed due to his knowledge of their consequence (عاقبته) on the Day of Resurrection (القيامة).

وَهُمْ مِنْ خَشْيَتِهِ ** (28) and they are fearful out of awe of Him. (meaning: out of His greatness (عظمته) and majesty (مهابته)) مُشْفِقُونَ ** (fearful), trembling (مرتعدون). The root of al-khashyah (الخشية) is fear accompanied by veneration (تعظيم), and for this reason, it is specifically attributed to the scholars (العلماء). And al-ishfaq (الإشفاق) is fear accompanied by concern (اعتناء). If it is transitive using (مِنْ), the meaning of fear (الخوف) in it is more apparent, and if it is transitive using (عَلَى), then the reverse is true.

وَمَنْ يَقُلْ مِنْهُمْ (referring to) from the angels or from the creatures إِنِّي إِلهٌ مِنْ دُونِهِ فَذلِكَ نَجْزِيهِ جَهَنَّمَ (29) And whoever of them says, 'I am indeed a god besides Him,' We shall requite him with hell. It is said that He intends by this the negation of Lordship (الرّبوبيّة) and the assertion of the negation of that (Lordship) from the creature, and the threatening of the polytheists (المشركين) by threatening the claimant of Lordship (الرّبوبيّة). And al-Qummi said: (It refers to) whoever claims that he is an Imam (إمام) while he is not an Imam. I say: Perhaps this interpretation (التأويل) and that exegesis (التَّفسير) (are related to His saying): كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ Thus do We requite the wrongdoers.

(30) أَوَلَمْ يَرَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (Do not the faithless see) Have they not known? And it was read without the waw (و) أَنَّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ كانَتا رَتْقاً فَفَتَقْناهُما (that the heavens and the earth were joined together, whereat We tore them apart?)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), that He was asked about this Ayah. He said: "Perhaps you claim that they were joined (رتقاً), stuck together, adhering to one another, and then one was torn apart from the other?" The questioner said: "Yes." He (peace be upon him) said: "Seek forgiveness from your Lord, for the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: كانَتا رَتْقاً (were joined together), means that the heaven was joined (رتقاً)—it did not send down rain—and the earth was joined (رتقاً)—it did not sprout grain. Then, when Allah created the creation and spread every creature therein, He tore open (فَتَقَ) the heaven with rain and the earth with the sprouting of grain." The questioner then said: "I bear witness that you are from the progeny of the Prophets and that their knowledge is incumbent upon you."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): what is similar to it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Him, that He was asked about them (the heavens and the earth). He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, sent Adam down to the earth, and the heaven was joined (رتقاً), not raining anything, and the earth was joined (رتقاً), not sprouting anything. Then, when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, accepted Adam's repentance, He commanded the heaven, and it dripped with clouds (الغمام). Then He commanded it, and it loosened its ropes (عزاليها). Then He commanded the earth, and it sprouted trees (الأشجار), bore fruits (الثمار), and split open with rivers (الأنّهار). That was their being joined (رتقها), and this is their being torn open (فتقها)."

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that He was asked about that. He said: "It is as He described Himself: كانَ عَرْشُهُ عَلَى الْماءِ (His Throne was upon the water). And the water was upon the air (الهواء), and the air is boundless (لا يحدّ). And on that day, nothing was created other than them. And the water on that day was sweet and palatable (عذب فراتٌ). Then, when Allah willed to create the earth, He commanded the winds (الرّياح), and they struck the water until it became waves (موجاً). Then it foamed (أزبد) and became a single foam (زبداً واحداً). He gathered it in the place of the House (البيت), then made it a mountain of foam (زبد). Then He spread (دحا) the earth from beneath it. Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ مُبارَكاً (Indeed, the first house to be set up for mankind is surely that which is at Bakka, blessed). Then the Lord, the Blessed and Exalted, remained as long as He willed. Then, when He willed to create the heaven, He commanded the winds, and they struck the seas (البحور) until they foamed them up. From that wave and foam, from its midst, came forth a bright smoke (دخان ساطع) without fire. He created the heaven from it and placed therein the constellations (البروج), the stars (النّجوم), and the stations of the sun and the moon, and made them run in the orbit (الفلك). The heaven was green, the color of green water, and the earth was dusty (غبراً), the color of sweet water. And they were both joined (مرتوقتين), having no doors. And the earth had no doors, which is the vegetation (النّبت), and the heaven did not rain upon it. So He tore open (فَتَقَ) the heaven with rain and tore open the earth with vegetation. That is His saying: أَوَلَمْ يَرَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (Do not the faithless see) the Ayah."

وَجَعَلْنا مِنَ الْماءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍ (and We made every living thing from water.) And We created every living creature from water, like His saying: وَاللهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَابَّةٍ مِنْ ماءٍ (And Allah has created every animal from water.) because it is the greatest of its materials, and due to its excessive need for it and its benefit from it directly. Or, We made every living thing alive by means of water, without which it cannot live. Al-Qummi said: He attributed every thing to water, but He did not attribute water to anything else.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): similar to it.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that He was asked about the taste of water. He said: "The taste of water is the taste of life."

In al-Majma', al-Ayyashi, and Qurb al-Isnad, similar to it, with the addition: Allah the Almighty said: وَجَعَلْنا مِنَ الْماءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍ أَفَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ (and We made every living thing from water. Will they not then believe?) despite the clarity of the signs (الآيات).

(٣١) وَجَعَلْنا فِي الْأَرْضِ رَواسِيَ firmly fixed ones أَنْ تَمِيدَ بِهِمْ out of dislike that it should incline with them وَجَعَلْنا فِيهَا فِجاجاً سُبُلاً wide paths لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ to their interests/benefits. وَجَعَلْنَا فِي الْأَرْضِ رَوَاسِيَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِهِمْ وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا فِجَاجًا سُبُلًا لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (٣١) (31) And We cast in the earth firm mountains, lest it should shake with them, and We made in it broad ways as routes, so that they may be guided.

(٣٢) وَجَعَلْنَا السَّماءَ سَقْفاً مَحْفُوظاً from falling, vanishing, and dissolving until the known time by His will, like the Almighty's saying: وَيُمْسِكُ السَّماءَ أَنْ تَقَعَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ** (And He holds the sky from falling on the earth, except by His leave.) And His saying: إِنَّ اللهَ يُمْسِكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ أَنْ تَزُولا (Indeed, Allah holds the heavens and the earth lest they cease to exist.) And al-Qummi means [it is preserved] from the devils, meaning they do not eavesdrop. وَهُمْ عَنْ آياتِها its states/conditions which indicate the perfection of His power, His greatness, and the ultimate extent of His knowledge and wisdom مُعْرِضُونَ not reflecting. وَجَعَلْنَا السَّمَاءَ سَقْفًا مَّحْفُوظًا وَهُمْ عَنْ آيَاتِهَا مُعْرِضُونَ** (٣٢) (32) And We made the sky a preserved roof, yet they are disregardful of its signs.

(٣٣) وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ ** (33) And He is the One who created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon. A clarification of some of those signs. كُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ** (Each one swims in an orbit): They hasten with the hastening of the swimmer in the water.

(٣٤) وَما جَعَلْنا لِبَشَرٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ الْخُلْدَ أَفَإِنْ مِتَّ فَهُمُ الْخالِدُونَ (34) And We did not appoint immortality for any human before you. So, if you die, will they be immortal?

(٣٥) كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ (35) Every soul shall taste death. In al-Qummi (التفسير): When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informed His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of what would befall his Household (أهل بيته) after him (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), and of the claim to the Caliphate (الخلافة) by those who claimed it instead of them, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم became grieved. So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down this verse. And it was said that it was revealed when they said: نَتَرَبَّصُ بِهِ رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ ** (We await for him the vicissitudes of fortune). A narration concerning the difference between death (الموت) and killing (القتل) has already preceded during the exegesis of this verse from Sūrat Āl ʿImrān. وَنَبْلُوكُمْ ** (And We test you): We treat you with the treatment of those who test (المختبرين). بِالشَّرِّ وَالْخَيْرِ ** (with the bad and the good): with afflictions (البلايا) and blessings (النّعم). فِتْنَةً ** (as a trial): a testing (ابتلاء). وَإِلَيْنا تُرْجَعُونَ ** (and to Us you shall be brought back): So We shall recompense you according to the patience (الصبر) and gratitude (الشكر) found in you.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام fell ill, and his brothers visited him. They asked: "How do we find you, O Commander of the Faithful?" He replied: "In badness (بشرّ)." They said: "This is not the speech of one like you." He said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: وَنَبْلُوكُمْ بِالشَّرِّ وَالْخَيْرِ فِتْنَةً ** (And We test you with the bad and the good, as a trial). The good (الخير) is health (الصحة) and wealth (الغنى), and the bad (الشر) is sickness (المرض) and poverty (الفقر)."

(٣٦) وَإِذا رَآكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُواً أَهذَا الَّذِي يَذْكُرُ آلِهَتَكُمْ ** (When the faithless see you, they take you only in derision: 'Is this the one who mentions your gods [with contempt]?') meaning, with ill وَهُمْ بِذِكْرِ الرَّحْمنِ هُمْ كافِرُونَ ** (while they are deniers of the remembrance of the All-beneficent.) So they are more deserving that derision be made of them. (٣٧) خُلِقَ الْإِنْسانُ مِنْ عَجَلٍ ** (Man was created of haste.) As if he was created from it due to the excessiveness of his haste (استعجاله) and the lack of his steadfastness (ثباته). Al-Qummi said: When Allah caused the Spirit (الرُّوح) to flow into Adam from his feet, and it reached his knees, he wanted to stand but was unable. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), said: خُلِقَ الْإِنْسانُ مِنْ عَجَلٍ. (Man was created of haste.) And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that which is close to it (in meaning). And in Nahj al-Balagha: Beware of haste (العجلة) in affairs before their proper time, and of falling short in them when they are possible—the narration continues. سَأُرِيكُمْ آياتِي فَلا تَسْتَعْجِلُونِ ** (Soon I shall show you My signs, so do not ask Me to hasten [them].) by bringing them.

(٣٨) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (38) And they say, 'When will this promise be, if you are truthful?' They mean the Prophet (النبيّ) and his companions (أصحابه). (٣٩) لَوْ يَعْلَمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا حِينَ لا يَكُفُّونَ عَنْ وُجُوهِهِمُ النَّارَ وَلا عَنْ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ ** (39) If only the faithless knew the time when they will not be able to ward off the Fire from their faces, nor from their backs, and they will not be helped! The answer (الجواب) is omitted, meaning they would not have hastened [it] (لما استعجلوا). (٤٠) بَلْ تَأْتِيهِمْ بَغْتَةً ** (40) Rather it will come upon them suddenly and dumbfound them, and they will not be able to ward it off, nor will they be granted any respite. Suddenly (فجأة). فَتَبْهَتُهُمْ ** so it overwhelms them (فتغلبهم) or confuses them (تحيّرهم). فَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ رَدَّها وَلا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ ** they are granted respite (يمهلون). (٤١) وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (41) Certainly apostles were ridiculed before you, but what they ridiculed besieged those who scoffed at them. A consolation (تسلية) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. فَحاقَ بِالَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا مِنْهُمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** And His justice (وعدله) is that what they do will encompass them (يحيق بهم). (٤٢) قُلْ مَنْ يَكْلَؤُكُمْ ** (42) Say, 'Who guards you by night and day from the All-beneficent?' Rather, they are disregardful of the remembrance of their Lord. Protects you (يحفظكم). بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ مِنَ الرَّحْمنِ ** from His might (بأسه) if He intends [harm] for you. And in the word الرَّحْمن (the All-beneficent) there is an alert (تنبيه) that there is no protector (كالئ) other than His universal mercy (رحمته العامّة), and that His repulsion [of harm] by means of it is a respite (مهلة). بَلْ هُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِمْ مُعْرِضُونَ ** They do not bring Him to mind (لا يخطرونه ببالهم), let alone fear His might (بأسه).

(٤٣) أَمْ لَهُمْ آلِهَةٌ تَمْنَعُهُمْ مِنْ دُونِنا (43) Do they have gods who defend them against Us? Rather, do they have gods that prevent them from the punishment—a prevention that surpasses Our prevention—or from a punishment that originates from Us? لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَلا هُمْ مِنَّا يُصْحَبُونَ They cannot help themselves, nor will they be befriended by Us. This is a new commencement (استيناف) intended to invalidate what they believe, for indeed, he who is unable to help himself and is not accompanied by help from Allah, how can he help others?

بَلْ مَتَّعْنا هؤُلاءِ وَآباءَهُمْ حَتَّى طالَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْعُمُرُ ٤٤ (44) Rather, We provided these and their fathers with comforts until their lives were prolonged. This is a transition (اضراب) away from what they imagined, explaining the reason (الداعي) for their preservation, which is gradual enticement (الاستدراج) and enjoyment (التمتيع) through the lifespans (الاعمار) decreed for them. Or, it is a transition away from indicating the invalidity of their belief, by explaining what caused them to be deluded, so they assumed that they would remain in that state forever and that it was due to the condition they were upon. This latter interpretation is more consistent with what follows it. أَفَلا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّا نَأْتِي الْأَرْضَ نَنْقُصُها مِنْ أَطْرافِها ** Do they not see that We visit the land, reducing it from its edges? It is said that the land refers to the land of the disbelievers (الكفرة). نَنْقُصُها مِنْ أَطْرافِها (reducing it from its edges) is said to mean by giving the Muslims dominance over it. This is a depiction of what Allah causes to happen at the hands of the Muslims. أَفَهُمُ الْغالِبُونَ ٤٤ Are they then the victors? (44) [Are they the victors over] the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers? In al-Kafi and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding نَنْقُصُها ** (We reduce it): He said it means by the death of the scholars (العلماء). He said its reduction is the departure (ذهاب) of its scholar (عالمها). Its explanation has already passed in Surat al-Ra'd.

(٤٥) قُلْ إِنَّما أُنْذِرُكُمْ بِالْوَحْيِ ** by what has been revealed to me وَلا يَسْمَعُ الصُّمُّ الدُّعاءَ إِذا ما يُنْذَرُونَ ** (45) Say: 'I warn you only by the revelation.' But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned. The word الصم (the deaf) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) to indicate their willful deafness (تصامّهم) and their lack of benefit from what they hear. And it was read (وقرئ) as ولا تسمع الصم (and you do not make the deaf hear), addressing the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(46) وَلَئِنْ مَسَّتْهُمْ نَفْحَةٌ the slightest thing (أدنى شيء) مِنْ عَذابِ رَبِّكَ from that which they are warned about (من الّذي ينذرون به) لَيَقُولُنَّ يا وَيْلَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ they would call woe (الويل) upon themselves and confess to wrongdoing (الظلم) against themselves. (46) And if a whiff of the punishment of your Lord touches them, they will surely say, 'Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!'

(47) وَنَضَعُ الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ the justice (العدل) by which deeds (الأعمال) are weighed لِيَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ فَلا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْئاً of its right (حقّه) or of injustice (الظلم) وَإِنْ كانَ مِثْقالَ حَبَّةٍ And it was read with al-rafʿ (بالرفع) مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ أَتَيْنا بِها We shall bring it forth (أحضرناها). (47) We shall set up the just scales on the Day of Resurrection, and no soul will be wronged in the least. And if there be the weight of a mustard seed, We shall produce it, and sufficient are We as reckoners.

In al-Jawamiʿ, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He read atina (أتينا) with al-madd (بالمدّ) [lengthening the alif]. And al-Qummi [said]: meaning, We shall recompense for it (جازينا بها), and it is lengthened (ممدودة). وَكَفى بِنا حاسِبِينَ since there is no addition (لا مَزيد) to Our knowledge (علمنا) and Our justice (عَدلنا).

In al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "They are the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء)." And in another narration: "We are الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ. (the just scales)."

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in response to one for whom some of the Ayahs were confusing: "As for His saying: وَنَضَعُ الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ ** (We shall set up the just scales), it is the scale of justice (ميزان العدل) by which the creatures (الخلائق) will be judged on the Day of Resurrection. Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, will judge the creation (الخلق), some against others, by means of the scales (بالموازين)."

I say: The meaning of the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء) being scales (موازين), and the verification (تحقيق) of the meaning of the scale (الميزان), has already preceded from us in the exegesis of وَالْوَزْنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُ (And the weighing on that day is the truth) from Surat al-A'raf.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) in his discourse on admonition (الوعظ) and asceticism (الزّهد), he said: "Then the saying of Allah in the Book returned to the people of disobedience (المعاصي) and sins (الذّنوب). So He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلَئِنْ مَسَّتْهُمْ نَفْحَةٌ مِنْ عَذابِ رَبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ يا وَيْلَنا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ ** (And if a whiff of the punishment of your Lord touches them, they will surely say, 'Woe to us! We have indeed been wrongdoers!'). If you say, O people, that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, only meant the people of polytheism (أهل الشرك) by this, how can that be when He says: وَنَضَعُ الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ لِيَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ ** (We shall set up the just scales on the Day of Resurrection) [the rest of] the Ayah? Know, O servants of Allah, that the people of polytheism (أهل الشرك) will not have scales (الموازين) set up for them, nor will registers (الدواوين) be spread out for them. Rather, they will be gathered to Hell (جهنّم) in groups (زمراً). The scales (الموازين) are only set up and the registers (الدواوين) are only spread out for the people of Islam (أهل الإِسلام). So fear Allah, O servants of Allah."

(٤٨) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسى وَهارُونَ الْفُرْقانَ وَضِياءً وَذِكْراً لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** (48) Certainly We gave Moses and Aaron the Criterion, and a light and a reminder for the Godwary, meaning, the comprehensive Book, because of its being a separator (فارقاً) between truth (الحق) and falsehood (الباطل), and a light (ضياء) by which illumination is sought in the darknesses of confusion (الحيرة) and ignorance (الجهالة), and a reminder (ذكراً) by which the Godwary (المتّقون) are admonished.

(٤٩) الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَيْبِ وَهُمْ مِنَ السَّاعَةِ مُشْفِقُونَ ** (49) those who fear their Lord in secret, and are apprehensive of the Hour. Fearing (خائفون). (٥٠) وَهذا ذِكْرٌ مُبارَكٌ ** (50) This is a blessed reminder which We have sent down. And this Qur’an is a reminder whose goodness (خيره) is abundant. We have sent it down (أَنْزَلْناهُ) upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. Are you going to deny it? (أَفَأَنْتُمْ لَهُ مُنْكِرُونَ) An interrogative of reproach (استفهام توبيخ). (٥١) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا إِبْراهِيمَ رُشْدَهُ ** (51) Certainly We gave Abraham his rectitude before, and We knew him well. The guidance (الاهتداء) to the aspects of righteousness (الصلاح). And He attributed it to him to indicate that it is rectitude (رشد) befitting him, and that he possesses a great status (شأناً). before (مِنْ قَبْلُ): before Moses and Aaron (peace be upon them) or Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. and We knew him well (وَكُنَّا بِهِ عالِمِينَ): We knew that he was worthy of what We granted Him.

(٥٢) إِذْ قالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ ما هذِهِ التَّماثِيلُ الَّتِي أَنْتُمْ لَها عاكِفُونَ ** (52) When he said to his father and his people, 'What are these statues to which you are clinging?' (This is) a belittling of their status (شأنها) and a reprimand for their veneration of them, for the statue (التمثال) is a form (صورة) without a spirit (روح) in it. (٥٣) قالُوا وَجَدْنا آباءَنا لَها عابِدِينَ ** (53) They said, 'We found our fathers worshipping them.' So we imitated them. (٥٤) قالَ لَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمْ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** (54) He said, 'Certainly you and your fathers have been in manifest error.' Due to the lack of reliance of both groups (الفريقين) upon a proof (برهان). (٥٥) قالُوا أَجِئْتَنا بِالْحَقِّ أَمْ أَنْتَ مِنَ اللَّاعِبِينَ ** (55) They said, 'Have you brought us the truth, or are you one of the triflers?' It is as if, due to their finding it unlikely that their fathers were misguided (تضليل آبائهم), they thought that what he said was in the manner of jesting (الملاعبة). So they said: Are you saying it seriously (ابجدّ) or are you jesting with it? (٥٦) قالَ بَلْ رَبُّكُمْ رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ الَّذِي فَطَرَهُنَ ** (56) He said, 'Indeed your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who originated them.' This is a turning away (اضراب) from the notion that he is jesting, by establishing the proof (البرهان) for what he claimed. وَأَنَا عَلى ذلِكُمْ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ ** (meaning) from those who confirm it (المحقّقين له) and establish the proof for it (المبرهنين عليه), for the witness (الشاهِد) is one who confirms (تحقّق) the thing and preserves it (حفظه).

(٥٧) وَتَاللهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصْنامَكُمْ ** (57) And by Allah, I will certainly devise a plot against your idols I shall certainly exert myself in breaking them. And the word الكيد (plot/scheme), and what is contained in the تاء (the particle ت) of astonishment (التعجب), is due to the difficulty of the matter and its dependence upon a type of stratagem (الحيل). بَعْدَ أَنْ تُوَلُّوا مُدْبِرِينَ ** after you have turned your backs and gone away to your festival (عيد). And perhaps he said that secretly.

(٥٨) فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذاذاً ** pieces (قطاعاً), a verbal noun pattern (فِعَال) having the meaning of a passive participle (مَفْعُول), like debris (الحُطَام), derived from al-jadhdh (الجَذّ) which is cutting (القَطْع). And it was recited with a kasrah (بالكسر). إِلَّا كَبِيراً لَهُمْ ** for the idols لَعَلَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ يَرْجِعُونَ (58) He turned them into fragments, except for the biggest one of them, so that they might return to it. (٥٩) قالُوا ** when they returned مَنْ فَعَلَ هذا بِآلِهَتِنا إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ (59) They said, "Who has done this to our gods? He is indeed one of the wrongdoers." (٦٠) قالُوا سَمِعْنا فَتًى يَذْكُرُهُمْ ** criticizing them (يعيبهم) يُقالُ لَهُ إِبْراهِيمُ (60) They said, "We heard a youth mentioning them—he is called Abraham." (٦١) قالُوا فَأْتُوا بِهِ عَلى أَعْيُنِ النَّاسِ ** in their sight (بمرأىً منهم) لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْهَدُونَ ** to his deed or his saying (بِفعله أو قوله) (61) They said, "Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may bear witness." (٦٢) قالُوا ** when they brought him forward أَأَنْتَ فَعَلْتَ هذا بِآلِهَتِنا يا إِبْراهِيمُ (62) They said, "Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?" (٦٣) قالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ هذا فَسْئَلُوهُمْ إِنْ كانُوا يَنْطِقُونَ (63) He said, "No, the biggest one of them did it. Ask them, if they can speak." In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Abraham only said إِنْ كانُوا يَنْطِقُونَ ** (if they can speak), so if they speak, their biggest one did it, but if they do not speak, then their biggest one did nothing. So, they did not speak, and Abraham did not lie. In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He only قالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ ** (said: No, the biggest one of them did it) intending correction (الإِصلاح) and indicating that they cannot act (لا يفعلون). Then he said: "By Allah, they did not do it, and he did not lie."

(٦٤) فَرَجَعُوا إِلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** and they consulted their intellects فَقالُوا ** so some of them said to others **إِنَّكُمْ أَنْتُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** by worshipping that which neither speaks, nor harms, nor benefits, not [by wronging] the one whom you wronged. (64) Thereupon they returned to themselves and said, 'It is indeed you who are the wrongdoers.'

(٦٥) **ثُمَّ نُكِسُوا عَلى رُؤُسِهِمْ ** It was said: meaning they reverted to argumentation after they had become upright through consultation. He likened their return to falsehood to the bottom of a thing becoming superior to its top. لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ ما هؤُلاءِ يَنْطِقُونَ So how do you command asking them, while he [Abraham] intends the saying [of this truth]. (65) Then they were turned upside down: 'You certainly know that these do not speak,' they said.

(٦٦) قالَ أَفَتَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَنْفَعُكُمْ شَيْئاً وَلا يَضُرُّكُمْ ** (66) He said, 'Do you worship, instead of Allah, that which neither benefits you in any way nor harms you? This is a denunciation of their worship of them (the idols) after their admission that they are inanimate objects (جمادات) that neither benefit nor harm, for this contradicts divinity (الألوهية). (٦٧) أُفٍّ لَكُمْ وَلِما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (67) Fie on you and on what you worship instead of Allah! This is an expression of annoyance (تضجّر) from him (Ibrahim عليه السلام) due to their persistence in manifest falsehood (الباطل البيّن). And أُفٍّ is the sound of the annoyed person, and its meaning is pus (قيحاً) and foulness (نتناً). أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ ** (Do you not exercise your reason?) regarding the ugliness of your action (قبح صنيعكم). (٦٨) قالُوا ** (68) They said, resorting to harm (المضارّة) when they were unable to argue (المحاجّة). حَرِّقُوهُ ** (Burn him) for fire is the most terrifying thing with which one can be punished. وَانْصُرُوا آلِهَتَكُمْ ** (and vindicate your gods) by taking revenge for them إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فاعِلِينَ ** (if you are going to act!) if you are going to grant them a strong victory (نصراً مؤزراً).

(٦٩) قُلْنا يا نارُ كُونِي بَرْداً وَسَلاماً ** (69) We said, 'O fire, be cool and safe for Abraham!' Possessing coolness and safety, meaning: Be cool, a coolness that is not harmful, عَلى إِبْراهِيمَ. (for Abraham!) (٧٠) وَأَرادُوا بِهِ كَيْداً ** (70) They sought to scheme against him, A plot (مكراً) in harming him, فَجَعَلْناهُمُ الْأَخْسَرِينَ ** (but We made them the biggest losers.) More losing than every loser, as their endeavor reverted as a decisive proof (برهاناً قاطعاً) that they were upon falsehood (الباطل) and Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was upon the truth (الحق), necessitating an increase in his rank and their deserving of the severest punishment. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was thrown into the fire, he said: "O Allah, I ask You by the right of Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد) that You save me from it." So Allah made it cool and safe for him.

(٧١) وَنَجَّيْناهُ وَلُوطاً إِلَى الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (71) And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We have blessed for all the nations.)**

[This refers] to al-Sham (الشام). It was said that its general blessing is that most of the Prophets were sent forth in it, and their laws, which are the foundations of religious and worldly perfections and goodnesses, spread throughout the worlds. [It is also blessed] due to the abundance of blessings therein and the prevailing fertility.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said:

So when Ibrahim (peace be upon him) forbade them and argued against them regarding their worship of idols, but they did not desist, a festival of theirs arrived. Nimrud (نمرود) and all the people of his kingdom went out to their festival, and he disliked that Ibrahim (peace be upon him) should go out with him, so he entrusted him with the house of the idols. When they left, Ibrahim (peace be upon him) went to some food and brought it into the house of their idols. He would approach idol after idol and say to it: "Eat and speak." If it did not answer him, he would take the axe (القدوم) and break its hand and leg, until he had done that to all the idols. Then he hung the axe around the neck of the largest one among them, which was in the front. When the king and those with him returned from the festival, they looked at the broken idols and said:

**مَنْ فَعَلَ هذا بِآلِهَتِنا إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ قالُوا سَمِعْنا فَتًى يَذْكُرُهُمْ يُقالُ لَهُ إِبْراهِيمُ ** (Who has done this to our gods? He is indeed one of the wrongdoers! They said, 'We heard a youth mentioning them, who is called Abraham.')

—and he is the son of Azar (آزر). So they brought him to Nimrud. Nimrud said to Azar: "You betrayed me and concealed this child from me!" He replied: "O King, this was the doing of his mother," and mentioned that she was responsible for concealing him. So Nimrud summoned the mother of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and said to her: "What compelled you to conceal the matter of this youth from me until he did to our gods what he did?" She said: "O King, it was out of consideration for your subjects." He asked: "And how was that?" She replied: "I saw you killing the children of your subjects, and this was destroying the lineage. So I said: 'If this is the one he seeks, I will hand him over to him so he may kill him and cease killing the children of the people. If it is not him, then our child remains for us.' Now you have found him, so do as you wish, and cease killing the children of the people." He approved of her opinion. Then he said to Ibrahim (peace be upon him):

**مَنْ فَعَلَ هذا بِآلِهَتِنا ** (Who has done this to our gods?)

"O Ibrahim?" Ibrahim said: فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ هذا فَسْئَلُوهُمْ إِنْ كانُوا يَنْطِقُونَ (This largest one of them did it. So ask them, if they can speak.)

Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, their largest one did not do it, and Ibrahim did not lie." It was asked: "How is that?" He replied: "He only said: فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ هذا ** (This largest one of them did it) if they can speak. And if they did not speak, then this largest one of them did nothing."

So Nimrud consulted his people concerning Ibrahim (peace be upon him), and they said: قَالُوا حَرِّقُوهُ وَانْصُرُوا آلِهَتَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فَاعِلِينَ (They said, 'Burn him and help your gods, if you are going to do anything.')

Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "The Pharaoh (فرعون) of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and his companions were not guided (لِغَيْرِ رُشْدَة), for they said to Nimrud: حَرِّقُوهُ وَانْصُرُوا آلِهَتَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فَاعِلِينَ (Burn him and help your gods, if you are going to do anything.) But the Pharaoh (فرعون) of Musa (Moses) and his companions were guided (لِرُشْدَة), for when he consulted his companions concerning Musa:

**قالُوا أَرْجِهْ وَأَخاهُ وَأَرْسِلْ فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ ساحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ ** (They said, 'Put him and his brother off, and send summoners to the cities, to bring you every knowing sorcerer.')

So Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was imprisoned, and firewood was gathered for him. Until the day arrived when Nimrud was to cast Ibrahim (peace be upon him) into the fire, Nimrud and his soldiers came forth. A structure had been built for Nimrud from which he could look at Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and see how the fire consumed him. Then Iblis (إبليس) came and made the catapult (المنجنيق) for them, because no one was able to approach the fire. And if a bird passed through the air, it would burn. So Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was placed in the catapult. Then his father came and slapped him once, saying to him: "Go back on what you are doing!" And the Lord sent down angels to the lowest heaven, and nothing remained except that it pleaded to its Lord. The Earth said: "O Lord, there is no one on my surface who worships You other than him, yet he is being burned!" The angels said: "O Lord, Your intimate friend (خليل), Ibrahim (peace be upon him), is being burned!" Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "Indeed, if he calls upon Me, I will suffice him." And Jibra'il (peace be upon him) said: "O Lord, Your intimate friend, Ibrahim (peace be upon him), is being burned! There is no one on Earth who worships You other than him, yet You have given his enemy power over him to burn him with fire!" He said: "Be silent! Only a servant like you, who fears loss, says this. He is My servant; I take him when I wish, but if he calls upon Me, I will answer him." So Ibrahim (peace be upon him) called upon his Lord with Surat al-Ikhlas (سورة الإخلاص): "O Allah, O the One (الواحد), O the Single (الأحد), O the Self-Sufficient (الصمد), O He Who:

**لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُواً أَحَدٌ ** (has not begotten, nor has He been begotten, nor has He any equal.)

Save me from the fire by Your mercy!" He said: So Jibra'il met him in the air while he was placed in the catapult, and he said: "O Ibrahim, do you have any need of me?" Ibrahim said: "As for you, no. But as for the Lord of the worlds, yes." So he handed him a ring upon which was inscribed: لا اله إلّا الله محمّد رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله (There is no god except Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, may Allah bless him and his family). "I have leaned my back upon Allah, I have entrusted my affair to Allah, and I have committed my matter to Allah." Then Allah revealed to the fire: كُونِي بَرْداً ** (Be cool). Ibrahim's teeth chattered from the cold until He said: سَلاماً عَلى إِبْراهِيمَ ** (a safety for Abraham.) (peace be upon him). And Jibra'il descended and sat with him, conversing with him in the fire. Nimrud looked at him and said: "Whoever takes a god, let him take a god like the God of Ibrahim!" Then a great man among Nimrud's companions said: "I have resolved upon the fire that it shall not burn him!" A column of fire shot out towards the man and burned him. So Lot (لوط) believed in him and emigrated to al-Sham (الشام). Nimrud looked at Ibrahim (peace be upon him) in a green garden within the fire, with an old man conversing with him, and he said to Azar: "O Azar, how honored is your son by his Lord!" He said: And the gecko (الوزغ) was blowing into the fire of Ibrahim (peace be upon him), while the frog (الضّفدع) was carrying water to extinguish the fire with it. He said: And when Allah the Almighty said to the fire: كُونِي بَرْداً وَسَلاماً ** (Be cool and safe), the fire did not work in the world for three days. Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: وَأَرادُوا بِهِ كَيْداً فَجَعَلْناهُمُ الْأَخْسَرِينَ ** (They sought to scheme against him, but We made them the biggest losers.) So Allah said: وَنَجَّيْناهُ وَلُوطاً إِلَى الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We have blessed for all the nations) — referring to al-Sham (الشام) and the fertile region (سواد) of al-Kufa (الكوفة).

In al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), is what is close to the beginning of this narration, with omissions and abbreviation.

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), is what is close to its end, likewise.

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Ibrahim (peace be upon him) broke the idols of Nimrud, Nimrud commanded that he be bound, and he commanded a pit (حَيْر) to be prepared for him, and firewood was gathered for him, and fire was kindled in it to burn him. Then Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was cast into the fire to burn him. Then they withdrew from it until the fire subsided. Then they looked down upon the pit, and behold, Ibrahim was safe, released from his bonds. Nimrud was informed of his news. So he commanded that Ibrahim be exiled from his lands and that he be prevented from leaving with his livestock and wealth. Ibrahim (peace be upon him) then argued with them, saying: "If you take my livestock and my wealth, then it is my right upon you that you return to me what has passed of my life in your lands." They took their dispute to Nimrud's judge. The judge ruled against Ibrahim, that he must hand over to them everything he had acquired in their lands, and he ruled against Nimrud's companions, that they must return to Ibrahim (peace be upon him) what had passed of his life in their lands. Nimrud was informed of this, so he commanded them to let him go, along with his livestock and wealth, and to expel him. He said: "If he remains in your lands, he will corrupt your religion and harm your gods."

(٧٢) وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ إِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ نافِلَةً (72) (And We gave him Isaac, and Jacob as a supererogatory gift) In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "The son of the son (ولد الولد) is a supererogatory gift (نافِلَةٌ)." And al-Qummi, regarding نافِلَةً , said: "The son of the son (ولد الولد)," and he is Jacob (ع). وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنا صالِحِينَ (and We made each of them righteous)

(٧٣) وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً ** (73) And We made them imams who are followed يَهْدُونَ ** guiding the people to the truth بِأَمْرِنا by Our command. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Imams (الأئمّة) in the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, are two types of Imams (امامان). Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنا ** (And We made them imams, guiding by Our command), not by the command of the people. They prioritize what Allah has commanded before their own command, and the judgment of Allah before their own judgment. He (peace be upon him) said: وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ ** (And We made them imams calling to the Fire). They prioritize their command before the command of Allah and their judgment before the judgment of Allah, and they follow their desires (أهوائهم) contrary to what is in the Book of Allah. وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْهِمْ فِعْلَ الْخَيْراتِ وَإِقامَ الصَّلاةِ وَإِيتاءَ الزَّكاةِ ** and We revealed to them the performance of good deeds, the maintenance of prayer and the giving of zakat. This is an instance of joining the specific (الخاصّ) to the general (العامّ). وَكانُوا لَنا عابِدِينَ ** and they were Our exclusive worshippers. They were monotheists (موحّدين), sincere (مخلصين) in worship, and for this reason, the prepositional phrase (الصلة) was placed first.

(٧٤) وَلُوطاً آتَيْناهُ حُكْماً وَعِلْماً وَنَجَّيْناهُ مِنَ الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي كانَتْ تَعْمَلُ الْخَبائِثَ ** (74) And Lot—We gave him judgment and knowledge, and delivered him from the town which was committing wicked deeds. القمّيّ said: They used to have intercourse with men. إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ فاسِقِينَ ** Indeed, they were a wicked, transgressing people. (٧٥) وَأَدْخَلْناهُ فِي رَحْمَتِنا إِنَّهُ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (75) And We admitted him into Our mercy; indeed, he was one of the righteous. (٧٦) وَنُوحاً إِذْ نادى ** (76) And Noah, when he called out when he called upon Allah against his people for destruction مِنْ قَبْلُ ** before that, before those who were mentioned فَاسْتَجَبْنا لَهُ ** so We responded to him. His supplication فَنَجَّيْناهُ وَأَهْلَهُ مِنَ الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ Then We delivered him and his family from the great distress, the intense grief, which is the harm inflicted by his people and the flood (الطوفان).

(77) وَنَصَرْناهُ (We made him victorious) مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ فَأَغْرَقْناهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (And We helped him against the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a wicked lot, so We drowned them all) due to their denial of the truth and their immersion in evil.

(78) وَداوُدَ وَسُلَيْمانَ إِذْ يَحْكُمانِ فِي الْحَرْثِ (the cultivated land or the vineyard) إِذْ نَفَشَتْ فِيهِ غَنَمُ الْقَوْمِ (grazed upon it at night). وَكُنَّا لِحُكْمِهِمْ (the judgment of the judges and the litigants) شاهِدِينَ (And David and Solomon, when they gave judgment concerning the tillage, when the sheep of the people grazed therein. And We were witnesses to their judgment)

(79) فَفَهَّمْناها سُلَيْمانَ وَكُلًّا آتَيْنا حُكْماً وَعِلْماً (So We gave its understanding to Solomon, and to each We gave judgment and knowledge)

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had revealed to the Prophets before David, up until Allah sent David, that if any sheep grazed (نفشت) in the tillage, the owner of the tillage was entitled to the lives (رقاب) of the sheep. Grazing (النّفش) only occurs at night, for the owner of the crop must guard his crop during the day, and the owner of the sheep must guard the sheep at night. So David judged according to what the Prophets before him had judged. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Solomon that if any sheep grazed (نفشت) in a crop, the owner of the crop is only entitled to what comes out of their bellies (i.e., the offspring). And this tradition (السّنة) continued after Solomon. This is the meaning of the Almighty's saying: وَكُلًّا آتَيْنا حُكْماً وَعِلْماً (and to each We gave judgment and knowledge). Thus, each of them judged according to the judgment of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): That which is close to it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah revealed to David: "Appoint a successor (وصيّاً) from your family, for it has preceded in My knowledge that I do not send a Prophet except that He has a successor from His family." David (peace be upon him) had several children, and among them was a young boy whose mother was with David, and he loved her. David entered upon her when the revelation came to him and said to her: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has revealed to me, commanding me to appoint a successor from my family." His wife said to him: "Let it be my son." He said: "That is what I desire." But what had preceded in the conclusive knowledge of Allah was that it was Solomon. So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed to David: "Do not hasten until My command comes to you." David did not wait long before two men came to him, disputing over sheep and a vineyard (الكرم). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to David (peace be upon him): "Gather your children, and whoever judges this case correctly, he shall be your successor after you." So David (peace be upon him) gathered them. When the two litigants presented their case, Solomon said: "O owner of the vineyard, when did this man's sheep enter your vineyard?" He said: "They entered it at night." Solomon said: "I have judged against you, O owner of the sheep, with the offspring of your sheep and their wool for this year." Then David (peace be upon him) said to him: "Why did you not judge with the lives (رقاب) of the sheep, when the scholars of the Children of Israel had appraised it, and the price of the vineyard was equal to the value of the sheep?" Solomon replied: "The vineyard was not uprooted from its foundation; rather, its fruit was eaten, and it will return next year." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to David (peace be upon him): "The judgment in this case is what Solomon has judged. O David, you desired one thing, and We desired another." David then entered upon his wife and said: "We desired one thing, but Allah desired another, and nothing occurred except what Allah desired. We are content with the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and we submit." Likewise, the successors (الأوصياء) are not permitted to transgress this matter and pass over its rightful owner to another.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: There was a man among the Children of Israel who had a vineyard (كرم), and the sheep of another man grazed (نفشت) in it at night, breaking and ruining it. The owner of the vineyard came to David and sought redress against the owner of the sheep. David (peace be upon him) said: "Go to Solomon so that he may judge between you." So they went to him. Solomon said: "If the sheep ate the root and the branch, then the owner of the sheep must hand over the sheep and what is in their bellies to the owner of the vineyard. But if they took the branch and did not take the root, then he must hand over their offspring to the owner of the vineyard." This was the judgment of David (peace be upon him), and he only intended to inform the Children of Israel that Solomon was his successor after him. They did not differ in judgment, for if their judgment had differed, He would have said: 'We were witnesses to their judgment.'

And in al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: They did not judge; rather, they were debating (يتناظران), and He gave the understanding to Solomon. And narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The judgment of David (peace be upon him) was the lives (رقاب) of the sheep, and what Allah gave Solomon understanding of was that the judgment for the owner of the tillage was the milk and the wool for that entire year.

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It was a vineyard whose clusters had appeared. David (peace be upon him) judged that the sheep belonged to the owner of the vineyard. Solomon said: "Something other than this, O Prophet of Allah, be gentle." He asked: "What is that?" Solomon said: "You hand over the vineyard to the owner of the sheep so that he may tend to it until it returns to its former state, and you hand over the sheep to the owner of the vineyard so that he may benefit from them. Then, when the vineyard returns to its former state, each of them returns his property to the other."

And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his Household): Solomon judged that the livestock must be guarded by their owners at night, and the tillage must be guarded by its owners during the day. وَسَخَّرْنا مَعَ داوُدَ الْجِبالَ يُسَبِّحْنَ (sanctifying Allah) with him. (And We subjected the mountains to David, glorifying with him)

And it is said: They moved quickly (يسرن) from swimming (السّباحة). وَالطَّيْرَ (and the birds)

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): David went out to recite the Psalms (الزّبور), and when he recited the Psalms, no mountain, nor stone, nor bird remained but that it responded to him.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): A Jew said to him: "This David (peace be upon him) wept over his sin until the mountains moved with him due to his fear." He (the Commander of the Faithful) said: "Indeed, he (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his Household) was like that." (The narration is long).

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He prayed two rak'ahs and glorified (Allah) in his prostration, and no tree nor clod of earth (مدر) remained but that they glorified (Allah) with him. وَكُنَّا فاعِلِينَ (of such things, so it is not unprecedented for Us, even if it is strange to you). (And We were doers)

(80) وَعَلَّمْناهُ صَنْعَةَ لَبُوسٍ لَكُمْ ** (80) And We taught him the making of coats of mail for you (It means) the making of the coat of mail (الدرع), and it (اللبوس) is originally the garment (اللباس). لِتُحْصِنَكُمْ مِنْ بَأْسِكُمْ ** to protect you from your violence. And it was recited with the tāʾ (التاء) and the nūn (النون). **فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ شاكِرُونَ ** Will you then be grateful? for that.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: Allah revealed to David (peace be upon him): "You are an excellent servant, were it not that you eat from the public treasury (بيت المال) and do not work with your hand at all." He said: So David (peace be upon him) wept for forty mornings. Then Allah revealed to the iron: "Be soft for My servant David." So Allah softened the iron for him. And he used to make a coat of mail (درعاً) every day and sell it for a thousand dirhams (درهم). So he made three hundred and sixty coats of mail (درعاً) and sold them for three hundred and sixty thousand, and became independent of the public treasury (بيت المال).

(٨١) وَلِسُلَيْمانَ ** (And to Solomon) And We subjected to him الرِّيحَ عاصِفَةً ** the wind, violently blowing intensely blowing (شديدة الهبوب). He traverses a great distance in a short period, as He said: غُدُوُّها شَهْرٌ وَرَواحُها شَهْرٌ تَجْرِي بِأَمْرِهِ. (its morning journey was a month, and its evening journey was a month, running by His command.) Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It runs from every side/direction. إِلى الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي بارَكْنا فِيها ** to the land which We have blessed in it He said: to Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس) and al-Sham (الشّام). وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عالِمِينَ and We are knowers of all things** So He makes it run according to what wisdom necessitates.

(٨٢) وَمِنَ الشَّياطِينِ مَنْ يَغُوصُونَ لَهُ ** (82) And among the devils were some who dived for him in the seas and extract its treasures وَيَعْمَلُونَ عَمَلاً دُونَ ذلِكَ ** and performed other works besides that. and they go beyond that to other works, such as building cities and palaces and inventing marvelous crafts (الصنايع الغريبة), due to the Almighty's saying: يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُ ما يَشاءُ مِنْ مَحارِيبَ وَتَماثِيلَ وَكُنَّا لَهُمْ حافِظِينَ ** They made for him whatever he wished of shrines and statues. And We were their guardians. from deviating from Our command or causing corruption, which is the requirement of their nature (جبلّتهم).

(83) وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نادى رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ الضُّرُّ ** (And [mention] Job, when he called out to his Lord, [saying,] 'Indeed distress has befallen me,) The word (الضُّرُّ) with fathah (ضَرّ) is common for all harm (ضرر), while with ḍammah (ضُرّ) it is specific to what affects the self, such as illness (مرض) and emaciation (هزال). وَأَنْتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ ** (and You are the most merciful of the merciful ones.') He described his Lord with the utmost degree of mercy after mentioning himself with that which necessitates it, and He sufficed with that instead of presenting the request, as a subtlety (لطفاً) in the supplication (السّؤال). It was said that he was Roman (روميّاً), from the progeny of Esau (عيص) son of Isaac (إسحاق). Allah made Him a prophet (استنبأه), and His family and wealth became numerous. Then Allah afflicted Him with the destruction of His children, the loss of His wealth, and illness in His body. And the mention of his story will come in Surat Ṣād, if Allah the Almighty wills.

(٨٤) فَاسْتَجَبْنا لَهُ فَكَشَفْنا ما بِهِ مِنْ ضُرٍّ (84) So We responded to him, and removed the distress that he had, by healing him from his illness. وَآتَيْناهُ أَهْلَهُ وَمِثْلَهُمْ مَعَهُمْ and We gave him his family and the like of them along with them, In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how he was given the like of them along with them. He said: "He revived for him those of his children who had died before that, at their appointed times (بآجالهم), similar to those who perished on that day." رَحْمَةً مِنْ عِنْدِنا as a mercy from Us upon him, وَذِكْرى and a reminder (وتذكرة) لِلْعابِدِينَ for the worshippers. In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Ayyub was afflicted for seven years without having committed a sin." And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The affliction of Ayyub with which he was tested in this world was only due to a blessing (لِنِعْمَةٍ) with which Allah had favored him, and he rendered thanks for it." (The rest of) the narrative (الحديث). And its completion will come, if Allah wills, in Surah Sad (سورة ص).
