(٨٥) وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِدْرِيسَ وَذَا الْكِفْلِ ** (85) and Ishmael, Idris, and Dhul-Kifl. He is Joshua son of Nun (يوشع بن نون). It was narrated in al-Uyun from al-Rida, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both), in the narrative of the Syrian (الشّامي). كُلٌ ** (85) All All of these مِنَ الصَّابِرِينَ ** (were among the patient) upon the hardships (مشاقّ) of religious obligations (التكاليف) and the severity (شدايد) of calamities (المصائب).

(٨٦) وَأَدْخَلْناهُمْ فِي رَحْمَتِنا ** (86) And We admitted them into Our mercy. النبوّة في الدنيا والنّعيم في الآخرة (The mercy is) Prophethood (النبوّة) in this world and bliss (النّعيم) in the Hereafter. إِنَّهُمْ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (Indeed, they were among the righteous.)

(٨٧) وَذَا النُّونِ ** (87) And [mention] the Man of the Fish وصاحب الحوت يونس بن متى And the Companion of the Fish (صاحب الحوت), Yunus ibn Matta. إِذْ ذَهَبَ مُغاضِباً ** when he went away in a rage, لقومه لمّا برم لطول دعوتهم وشدّة شكيمتهم وتمادي إصرارهم مهاجراً عنهم قبل أن يؤمر به كما سبق قصّته في سورته towards his people when he grew weary due to the length of his calling them, the severity of their obstinacy (شدّة شكيمتهم), and the persistence of their insistence (on disbelief), migrating away from them before he was commanded to do so, as his story preceded in his Surah (chapter). فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ and thought that We would not put him in distress. قيل اي لن نضيق عليه ولن نقضي عليه بالعقوبة من القدر أو لن نعمل فيه قدرتنا It was said: meaning, We would not restrict Him (Yunus) nor decree punishment upon him, derived from al-qadar (القدر), or (meaning) We would not execute Our power (قدرتنا) upon him. وقيل هو تمثيل لحاله بحال من ظنّ ان لن نقدر عليه في أمر مراغمة قومه من غير انتظار لأمرنا أو خطرة شيطانية سبقت الى وهمه فسمّي ظنّاً للمبالغة And it was said: This is a representation (تمثيل) of his state as the state of one who thought that We would not be able to overpower him (لن نقدر عليه) regarding the matter of vexing his people (مراغمة قومه) without awaiting Our command, or (it was) a satanic thought (خطرة شيطانية) that preceded into his mind, so it was called a 'thought' (ظنّاً) for emphasis (للمبالغة). فَنادى فِي الظُّلُماتِ أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ Then he cried out in the darkness, 'There is no god except You! You are immaculate! I have indeed been among الظَّالِمِينَ ** the wrongdoers.' قيل أي لنفسي بالمبادرة الى المهاجرة. It was said: meaning, (wrongdoer) to myself (لنفسي) by rushing to migrate (بالمبادرة الى المهاجرة).

وفي العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام : انه سئل عن هذه الآية فقال ذاك يونس بن متّى ذَهَبَ مُغاضِباً لقومه فَظَنَ بمعنى استيقن أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ أي لن نضيق عليه رزقه ومنه قول الله عزّ وجلّ وَأَمَّا إِذا مَا ابْتَلاهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُ أي ضيّق عليه وقتر فَنادى فِي الظُّلُماتِ ظلمة الليل وظلمة البحر وظلمة بطن الحوت أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ بتركي مثل هذه العبادة الّتي قد فرغتني لها في بطن الحوت فاستجاب الله وقال عزّ وجلّ فَلَوْ لا أَنَّهُ كانَ مِنَ الْمُسَبِّحِينَ لَلَبِثَ فِي بَطْنِهِ إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "That is Yunus ibn Matta. He went away in a rage towards his people. And he thought (فَظَنَ) means he became certain (استيقن). that We would not put him in distress (أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ), meaning, We would not restrict His provision (رزقه). And from this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَأَمَّا إِذا مَا ابْتَلاهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُ ** (But when He tests him and restricts his provision for him). meaning, He restricted it (ضيّق عليه) and made it scarce (وقتر). Then he cried out in the darkness (فَنادى فِي الظُّلُماتِ): the darkness of the night (ظلمة الليل), the darkness of the sea (ظلمة البحر), and the darkness of the belly of the fish (ظلمة بطن الحوت): 'There is no god except You! You are immaculate! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers.' (أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ) by abandoning such worship (العبادة) for which You have made me free (to perform) in the belly of the fish." So Allah responded to Him, and He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَلَوْ لا أَنَّهُ كانَ مِنَ الْمُسَبِّحِينَ لَلَبِثَ فِي بَطْنِهِ إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ** (And had he not been one of those who glorify [Allah], he would have remained in its belly till the day they will be resurrected.)

وفي رواية أخرى عنه عليه السلام : بعد تفسير لَنْ نَقْدِرَ بما ذكر ولو ظنّ ان الله لا يقدر عليه لكان قد كفر. In another narration from him (peace be upon him): after interpreting We would not put him in distress (لَنْ نَقْدِرَ) with what was mentioned, (he added): "Had he thought that Allah was incapable of Him, he would have disbelieved (لكان قد كفر)."

والقمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : في قوله وَذَا النُّونِ إِذْ ذَهَبَ مُغاضِباً يقول من أعمال قومه فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ يقول ظنّ ان لن نعاقب بما صنع. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: And [mention] the Man of the Fish when he went away in a rage, He says: (He was enraged) by the deeds of his people. and thought that We would not put him in distress. He says: He thought that We would not punish him for what he had done.

وعن الصادق عليه السلام : انه سئل ما كان سببه حتّى فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ قال وكّله إلى نفسه طرفة عين. And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what the reason was that led him to and thought that We would not put him in distress. He said: "He (Allah) entrusted him to his own self for the blink of an eye (طرفة عين)."

وعن النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله : انّما وكل الله يونس بن متّى إلى نفسه طرفة عين فكان منه ما كان. And from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Allah only entrusted Yunus ibn Matta to his own self for the blink of an eye (طرفة عين), and thus what happened, happened.

وعن الصادق عليه السلام : بعد ما ذكر من قصة يونس ما سبق في سورته قال فغضب يونس ومرّ على وجهه مغاضباً لله كما حكى الله عنه حتّى انتهى الى ساحل البحر فإذا سفينة قد شحنت الحديث ويأتي تمامه في سورة الصّافات إن شاء الله ويذكر فيه ما دعاه الى ندائه في الظلمات. And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): after mentioning what preceded in the Surah (chapter) regarding the story of Yunus, he said: "So Yunus became angry and went on his way, enraged at Allah, as Allah recounted about him, until he reached the seashore. There, he found a ship that had been loaded..." (the Hadith continues, and its completion will come in Surat al-Saffat, if Allah wills, and therein will be mentioned what prompted him to call out in the darknesses).

(٨٨) فَاسْتَجَبْنا لَهُ وَنَجَّيْناهُ مِنَ الْغَمِ ** (88) So We responded to him and delivered him from the distress. By the whale casting him onto the shore, and Allah causing a tree of gourd (يقطين) to grow over him. وَكَذلِكَ نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** And thus do We deliver the faithful. from distresses when they call upon Allah therein with sincerity (الإخلاص). And it was recited with a single nūn (نون) and the jīm (جيم) intensified (تشديد).

In al-Faqih and al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): I am astonished at the one who is alarmed by four things, how he does not resort to four things—up to his (peace be upon him) saying: I am astonished at the one who is distressed (اغتم), how he does not resort to His Almighty saying: لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ (There is no god except You! Glory be to You! I have indeed been among the wrongdoers!) For I heard Allah say immediately following it: فَاسْتَجَبْنا لَهُ وَنَجَّيْناهُ مِنَ الْغَمِّ وَكَذلِكَ نُنْجِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (So We responded to him and delivered him from the distress. And thus do We deliver the faithful.) And it is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: There is no distressed person (مكروب) who supplicates with this supplication (الدّعاء) except that He responds to him.

(٨٩) وَزَكَرِيَّا إِذْ نادى رَبَّهُ رَبِّ لا تَذَرْنِي فَرْداً ** (89) And Zachariah, when he cried out to his Lord, 'My Lord! Do not leave me without an heir, meaning: alone (وَحِيداً) without a child to inherit from me, وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْوارِثِينَ ** and You are the best of inheritors.' for if You do not grant me someone to inherit from me, I do not care about it. (٩٠) فَاسْتَجَبْنا لَهُ وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ يَحْيى وَأَصْلَحْنا لَهُ زَوْجَهُ (90) So We responded to him, and We gave him John, and We reformed his wife for him. Al-Qummi said in his narration: She used not to menstruate, but then she menstruated. إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْراتِ ** Indeed, they were quick to [perform] good works, meaning: they hastened to the gates of goodness, وَيَدْعُونَنا رَغَباً وَرَهَباً and called Us with hope and fear. Al-Qummi said: hoping (رَاغِبِينَ) and fearing (رَاهِبِينَ). I SAY: Perhaps what is meant by the desire (الرغبة) is the desire for obedience, not for the reward (الثواب), and the fear (الرّهبة) is the fear of disobedience (المعصية), not of the punishment (العقاب), due to the elevated status (مقام) of the Prophets above that.

The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "My God, I did not worship You out of fear of Your Fire, nor out of greed for Your Paradise, but I found You worthy of worship, so I worshipped You."

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, people worship Allah in three ways. One class worships Allah desiring His reward (ثوابه); that is the worship of the greedy (الحرصاء), which is covetousness (الطمع). Others worship Him fearing the Fire; that is the worship of slaves (العبيد), which is fear (الرّهبة). But I worship Him out of love for Him; that is the worship of the noble (الكرام)." In some wordings, الاجراء (the hired workers) is used instead of الحرصاء (the greedy). You may also say that the friends of Allah (أولياء الله) may perform some deeds for the sake of Paradise and averting the Fire because their Beloved (Allah) loves that. This is the Commander of the Faithful, the Master of the Friends of Allah (سيّد الأولياء), who wrote a document concerning some of the wealth he endowed (وقفه). He began his document, after the Tasmiyah (saying بسم الله), with: "This is what the servant of Allah, Ali, has bequeathed and decreed concerning his wealth, seeking the Face of Allah, so that He may admit me thereby into Paradise, and avert the Fire from me thereby, and avert the Fire from me on the Day when faces will turn white and faces will turn black." Or you may say that the Paradise of the Friends of Allah is the meeting with Allah (لقاء الله) and nearness to Him, and their Fire is separation from Him and distance from Him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The desire (الرغبة) is that you face the sky with the palms of your hands, and the fear (الرّهبة) is that you turn the backs of your hands toward the sky. وَكانُوا لَنا خاشِعِينَ ** and were humble before Us. meaning: submissive (مُخْبِتِينَ) or perpetually fearful (دَائِمِينَ الْوَجَل). The meaning is that they attained from Allah what they attained through these characteristics (الخصال).

(٩١) وَالَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَها (91) And she who guarded her chastity, Al-Qummi said: Maryam (Mary) was not looked upon by anything. فَنَفَخْنا فِيها مِنْ رُوحِنا then We breathed into her of Our Spirit, The clarification of the meaning of the Spirit (الرّوح) has already preceded in Surat al-Hijr. وَجَعَلْناها وَابْنَها آيَةً لِلْعالَمِينَ and We made her and her son a sign for all the nations. For indeed, whoever reflects upon the state of the two of them will realize the perfection of the power of the Almighty Creator.

(٩٢) إِنَّ هذِهِ أُمَّتُكُمْ ** (Indeed, this community of yours) Your religion (ملّتكم), and it is the religion (ملّة) of Islam and Monotheism (التوحيد). أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (is one community) Not differing among the Prophets. وَأَنَا رَبُّكُمْ ** (and I am your Lord) There is no god (إله) for you other than Me. فَاعْبُدُونِ ** (so worship Me.) Not other than Me. (٩٣) وَتَقَطَّعُوا أَمْرَهُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (But they fragmented their affair among themselves) They became divided in religion (الدين) and made its affair (أمره) into distributed fragments (قطعاً موزّعة), each one belonging to the fragmented sects (الفرق المتجزّية). إِلَيْنا راجِعُونَ ** (to Us they will return.) So We shall recompense them. (٩٤) فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحاتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ ** (So whoever does righteous deeds and is a believer) In Allah and His Messengers. فَلا كُفْرانَ لِسَعْيِهِ ** (his endeavor shall not be denied) There shall be no waste (تضييع) of his endeavor. [The term كُفْران] was borrowed to mean the withholding of reward (الثواب), just as الشكر (gratitude) was borrowed to mean the granting of it. وَإِنَّا لَهُ ** (and We are indeed recording it for him.) For his endeavor (لسعيه), recording (كاتِبُونَ), confirming (مثبتون) in the scroll (صحيفة) of his deed (عمله).

(٩٥) وَحَرامٌ عَلى قَرْيَةٍ ** (95. And it is forbidden for a town) Impossible for its people, not conceivable from them. And it was read حِرْم (ḥirm) with the breaking of the ḥāʾ and the quiescence of the rāʾ. أَهْلَكْناها أَنَّهُمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ ** (whose people We have destroyed, that they will not return) It was said: meaning, their return to the world or to repentance is forbidden (حَرامٌ), and لا is not superfluous (مزيدة). And it was said: meaning, the non-return of them for recompense is forbidden (حَرامٌ), and it (أَنَّهُمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ) is the subject (مبتدأ) and حَرامٌ is its predicate (خبر).

In al-Faqih, in the Friday sermon of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Have you not seen those who have passed away from among you, they do not return, and those who remain behind from among you, they will not remain? Allah the Almighty said: وَحَرامٌ عَلى قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْناها أَنَّهُمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ (And it is forbidden for a town whose people We have destroyed, that they will not return)." And this refers to the first meaning, and it is supported by the reading with the kasrah (الكسر) among the anomalous readings (الشّواذّ), just as it also supports the second meaning. And the famous reading with the fatḥah (الفتح) supports the third meaning.

And al-Qummi narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them): They said: "Every town whose people Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, destroyed with punishment will not return in the Return (الرجعة)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Every town that Allah destroyed with punishment, they will not return."

(٩٦) حَتَّى إِذا فُتِحَتْ ** (96) Until when it is opened) And it was read with tashdid (doubling the letter). يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ ** (Ya'juj and Ma'juj), meaning their barrier (سدّهما). Al-Qummi said: When it is the end of time, Ya'juj and Ma'juj will emerge into the world and they will eat people. وَهُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ ** (And they from every hadab), meaning a raised part (نشز) of the earth. يَنْسِلُونَ ** (They hasten), meaning they hurry (يسرعون).

**حَتَّى إِذَا فُتِحَتْ يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ وَهُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنْسِلُونَ **
(Until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they swarm down from every elevation)

(٩٧) وَاقْتَرَبَ الْوَعْدُ الْحَقُّ فَإِذا هِيَ شاخِصَةٌ أَبْصارُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (And the true promise draws near, and behold, the eyes of the faithless will stare [in terror].) This is the apodosis (جواب الشرط) and the إذا (idhā) is for suddenness (للمفاجأة). يا وَيْلَنا ** (Woe to us!) is understood to be preceded by the word "saying" (بالقول). قَدْ كُنَّا فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ هذا ** (We were certainly oblivious of this) means: We did not know that it was true (حقّ). Rather, بَلْ كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ (we were wrongdoers) to ourselves by neglecting contemplation (بالإخلال بالنظر) and transgressing against the warnings (والاعتداء بالنّذر).

(٩٨) إِنَّكُمْ وَما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ ** (Indeed you and what you worship besides Allah are the fuel of hell.) It is thrown into it (Hell) and it is ignited by it, derived from (حَصَبَهُ يَحْصِبُهُ) when he throws gravel (الحَصْبَاء) at him. And al-Qummi (said): They will be hurled into it with a hurling. And in al-Majma': The reading of Ali (عليه السلام) is (حَطَب) with the letter (الطاء). أَنْتُمْ لَها وارِدُونَ ** (you will enter it.) The (اللام) (in لَها) is substituted for (على) to denote specification (للاختصاص) and to indicate that their entering (وُرُودَهُمْ) is for its sake.

(٩٩) لَوْ كانَ هؤُلاءِ آلِهَةً ما وَرَدُوها وَكُلٌّ فِيها خالِدُونَ ** (99) Had these been gods, they would not have entered it, and all of them will remain in it [forever]. There is no escape for them from it. (١٠٠) لَهُمْ فِيها زَفِيرٌ ** (100) For them therein is sighing, and they will not hear therein. A moan and heavy breathing وَهُمْ فِيها لا يَسْمَعُونَ In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both), that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will bring forth on the Day of Resurrection everything that was worshipped besides Him, whether it be the sun, or the moon, or anything else. Then He will ask every human being about what he used to worship. Everyone who worshipped other than Allah will say: 'Our Lord, we used to worship them so that they might bring us nearer to You in proximity (زلفى).' He said: Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, will say to the angels: 'Take them and what they used to worship to the Fire.' And those whom I have exempted are excluded, for those will be kept far away from it." And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "When the Day of Resurrection comes, the sun and the moon will be brought forth in the form of two bulls, and they will be cast, along with those who worshipped them, into the Fire. That is because they were worshipped as a matter of obligation (فرضياً)." I say: The interpretation (تأويل) of this narrative will come in Surat al-Rahman. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When this Ayah was revealed, the people of Mecca felt great distress because of it. Then Abdullah ibn al-Ziba'ra entered upon them while the disbelievers of Quraysh were discussing this Ayah. Ibn al-Ziba'ra asked: "Did Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله speak with this Ayah?" They said: "Yes." Ibn al-Ziba'ra said: "If he acknowledges it, I shall surely defeat him in argument." So, they brought the two of them together. He said: "O Muhammad, do you see the Ayah which you recited just now? Is it specifically about us and our gods, or about the nations and their gods?" He said: "Yes, it is about you and your gods, and about the nations and their gods, except for those whom Allah has exempted." Ibn al-Ziba'ra said: "By Allah, I have defeated you in argument! Do you not praise Jesus (peace be upon him) highly, and you know that the Christians worship Jesus and his mother? And a group of people worship the angels. Will not these be in the Fire along with the gods?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "No." So, Quraysh raised a clamor and laughed. Quraysh said: "Ibn al-Ziba'ra has defeated you in argument!" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "You have spoken falsehood. Did I not say: 'Except for those whom Allah has exempted,' which is His saying: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنى أُولئِكَ عَنْها مُبْعَدُونَ ** (Indeed, those for whom the good has preceded from Us, those will be kept far away from it) up to His saying: أَنْفُسُهُمْ خالِدُونَ.

(١٠١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنى ** (Indeed those for whom the good promise has already gone forth from Us) meaning, the good quality (الخصلة الحسنى). أُولئِكَ عَنْها مُبْعَدُونَ. (will be kept far away from it.) Al-Qummi means the angels and Jesus son of Mary (عليه السلام). (١٠٢) لا يَسْمَعُونَ حَسِيسَها ** (They will not hear its slightest sound) meaning, its sound (صوتها) which is perceived (يُحَسّ بِهِ). وَهُمْ فِي مَا اشْتَهَتْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ خالِدُونَ (and they will be immortal in what their souls desire.) (١٠٣) لا يَحْزُنُهُمُ الْفَزَعُ الْأَكْبَرُ وَتَتَلَقَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ هذا يَوْمُكُمُ الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ ** (The great terror will not grieve them, and the angels will receive them: 'This is your day which you were promised.') meaning, in the world (في الدنيا).

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): He said to Ali (عليه السلام): "O Ali, you and your followers (شيعتك) will be at the Pond (الحوض), giving drink to those whom you love and preventing those whom you dislike. You are the secure ones on the Day of the Great Terror (يوم الفزع الأكبر) in the shade of the Throne (العرش). People will be terrified, but you will not be terrified; people will grieve, but you will not grieve. And concerning you, this verse was revealed: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنى ** (Indeed those for whom the good promise has already gone forth from Us) [the rest of] the verse. And concerning you, the verse لا يَحْزُنُهُمُ الْفَزَعُ الْأَكْبَرُ ** (The great terror will not grieve them) was revealed."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (عليه السلام), he said: "Indeed, Allah will resurrect our followers (شيعتنا) on the Day of Resurrection, despite the sins they may have committed or otherwise, with their faces brightened (مبيضّة وجوههم), their private parts covered (مستورة عوراتهم), their fear secured (آمنة روعتهم). The paths (الموارد) will be made easy for them, and the hardships (الشّدائد) will be removed from them. They will ride she-camels (نوقاً) made of ruby (ياقوت), and they will continuously circle within Paradise (خلال الجنّة). Upon them will be a net (شرك) of glittering light (نور يتلألؤ). Tables (الموائد) will be set for them, and they will continuously eat while the people are undergoing the Reckoning (الحساب). And this is the saying of Allah, the Blessed and Exalted: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُمْ مِنَّا الْحُسْنى ** (Indeed those for whom the good promise has already gone forth from Us) [the rest of] the verse."

يَوْمَ نَطْوِي السَّماءَ كَطَيِّ السِّجِلِّ لِلْكُتُبِ ** (The day We shall roll up the heaven, just as a scroll is rolled up for writings) It is said: like the rolling up of the scroll (الطومار) for the purpose of writing, or for what is written therein. And it was read with the plural (على الجمع), meaning: for the many meanings written therein. And al-Qummi said: السِّجِلِ ** is the name of the angel who rolls up the writings (الكتب). And the meaning of Our rolling them up (نطويها) is that We annihilate them (نفنيها), so they turn into smoke, and the earth turns into fire. كَما بَدَأْنا أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍ نُعِيدُهُ وَعْداً عَلَيْنا (As We originated the first creation, We shall bring it back. That is a promise binding on Us), meaning: its fulfillment (إنجازه) is incumbent upon Us. إِنَّا كُنَّا فاعِلِينَ (Indeed, We have been doing it) — We shall certainly do that.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He said: "They will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection naked, barefoot, and unarmed (عُزلاً)." **كَما بَدَأْنا أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍ نُعِيدُهُ ** (As We originated the first creation, We shall bring it back) — the Ayah.

(١٠٥) وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنا فِي الزَّبُورِ ** (And certainly We wrote in the Psalms) — meaning, in the book of David (peace be upon him) — مِنْ بَعْدِ الذِّكْرِ. (after the Reminder). Al-Qummi said: All the Books (الكتب) are a Reminder (ذكر). أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُها عِبادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ ** (that My righteous servants shall inherit the earth). He said: [They are] al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) and his companions. He said: And al-Zabur (الزَّبُورِ) contains epics (ملاحم), praise (تحميد), glorification (تمجيد), and supplication (دعاء). And in another narration: And Allah sent down upon him—meaning upon David (داود)—al-Zabur (الزَّبُورِ), which contains praise (تحميد), glorification (تمجيد), supplication (دعاء), news of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and the Imams (الأئمّة) from their progeny (peace be upon them), news of the Return (الرّجعة), and mention of al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah: What is الزَّبُورِ ** (the Psalms) and what is الذِّكْرِ ** (the Reminder)? He said: الذِّكْرِ ** (The Reminder) is with Allah, and al-Zabur (الزَّبُورِ) is that which was sent down upon David (peace be upon him). And every Book (كتاب) that was sent down is with the People of Knowledge (اهل العلم), and we are they. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُها عِبادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ ** (that My righteous servants shall inherit the earth). He said: They are the companions of al-Mahdi (peace be upon him) at the end of time. The author of al-Majma' said: What supports this is that which has been narrated by both the Khass (الخاصّ) and the Amm (العام) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He said: "If there remained of the world but a single day, Allah would prolong that day until He sends forth a man from the People of My House (أهل بيتي) who will fill the earth with equity and justice, just as it was filled with oppression and tyranny."

(106) إِنَّ فِي هذا لَبَلاغاً لِقَوْمٍ عابِدِينَ (There is indeed a sufficiency in this for a people who worship.) The Almighty's saying: إِنَّ فِي هذا (There is indeed in this) — meaning, in what has been mentioned of the narratives (الأخبار) and admonitions (المواعظ) — لَبَلاغاً (a sufficiency) — meaning, a sufficiency for reaching the desired goal (البغيّة) — لِقَوْمٍ عابِدِينَ (for a people who worship) — whose concern (همّهم) is worship (العبادة) and not mere habit (العادة).

(107) وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعالَمِينَ (We have not sent you except as a mercy to all the nations.) And the Almighty's saying: وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعالَمِينَ (We have not sent you except as a mercy to all the nations) — because what You were sent with is a cause for their happiness (إسعادهم) and necessitates the rectification (صلاح) of their worldly life (معاشهم) and their afterlife (معادهم). And His being a mercy to the disbelievers (الكفّار) is that through Him, they were secured from being swallowed by the earth (الخسف), metamorphosis (المسخ), and the punishment of utter eradication (عذاب الاستيصال).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a narration responding to some of the heretics (الزنادقة): "As for His saying to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: وَما أَرْسَلْناكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِلْعالَمِينَ (We have not sent you except as a mercy to all the nations), and yet you see the people of the religions (الملل) that oppose faith (الإيمان), and those like them among the disbelievers (الكفّار), persisting in their disbelief until this moment. If He were a mercy upon them, they would all have been guided and saved from the punishment of the Blaze (عذاب السعير)."

"Indeed, Allah, blessed and exalted is His Name, only meant by that that He made him a means (سبيلاً) for warning (إنذار) the inhabitants of this abode (هذه الدار). This is because the Prophets before him were sent with explicit declaration (بالتصريح), not with allusion (بالتعريض). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was among them when He proclaimed the command of Allah. If his people responded to Him, they were safe, and the inhabitants of their abode were safe from the rest of creation. But if they opposed Him, they perished, and the inhabitants of their abode perished by the calamity (الآفة) with which their Prophet used to threaten them and warn them of its descent and arrival upon their land: whether it be swallowing by the earth (خسف), stoning (قذف), shaking (رجف), wind (ريح), earthquake (زلزلة), or other types of punishment (أصناف العذاب) by which the past nations (الأمم الخالية) perished."

"And Allah knew that our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Proofs (الحجج) on Earth possessed patience (الصبر) for what those who preceded them among the Prophets were unable to bear. So, He sent Him with allusion (بالتعريض) and not with explicit declaration (بالتصريح). And He established the Proof (حجّة) of Allah by allusion (تعريضاً), not by explicit declaration (تصريحاً), through His saying concerning His successor (وصيّه): 'Whosoever I am his Mawla (مولاه), this Ali is his Mawla (مولاه), and his status to me is like the status of Harun from Musa, except that there is no Prophet after me.' It is not characteristic (خليقة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, nor is it His nature (شيمته), to utter a saying that has no meaning. Therefore, it was incumbent upon the Ummah (الأمة) to know that since Prophethood (النبوّة) and brotherhood (الاخوّة) were present in the creation of Harun, but absent in the one whom the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله placed in his status, He had indeed appointed him as His successor (استخلفه) over His Ummah, just as Musa appointed Harun (peace be upon him) as successor, when He said to him: اخْلُفْنِي فِي قَوْمِي (38) (Be my successor among my people)" "Had He said to them: 'Do not assume the Imamate (الإمامة) except for this specific person, otherwise punishment will descend upon you,' the punishment would have come upon them, and the door of respite (الإنظار) and delay (الإمهال) would have been removed."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said to Jibra'il: "When this verse was revealed, did any part of this mercy reach you?" He replied: "Yes. I used to fear the outcome of the matter (عاقبة الامر), but I became secure through You when Allah praised me with His saying: ذِي قُوَّةٍ عِنْدَ ذِي الْعَرْشِ مَكِينٍ. (20) (possessing power, established with the Lord of the Throne,)"

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "If our Qa'im (قائمنا) were to rise, al-Humayra' (الحميراء) would be brought back until He administers the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) upon her, and until He takes vengeance for the daughter of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, Fatimah (peace be upon her), from her." It was asked: "Why will He administer the prescribed punishment upon her?" He replied: "Because of her slander (فريتها) against Umm Ibrahim (أمّ إبراهيم)." It was asked: "How is it that Allah delayed this for the Qa'im (peace be upon him)?" He replied: "Indeed, Allah, blessed and exalted is He, sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله as a mercy (رحمة), and He will send the Qa'im (peace be upon him) as vengeance (نقمة)."

(108) قُلْ إِنَّما يُوحى إِلَيَّ أَنَّما إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (Say, 'I am only inspired that your God is the One God.') Nothing is revealed to me except that your God is none other than one God, and that is because the primary objective (المقصود الأصلي) of His mission (بعثته) is confined to monotheism (التّوحيد). فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ ** (Will you then submit?') [Meaning:] Sincere in worship (العبادة) to Allah according to the dictates (مقتضى) of the revelation (الوحي).

In المناقب, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ ** (Will you then submit?): "The testament (الوصيّة) after me was revealed strictly enforced (مشدّدة)."

I say: And the meaning of the two [statements] is one, because opposing the testament (الوصيّة) is worship (عبادة) of caprice (الهوى) and Satan (الشيطان).

(١٠٩) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** (109) But if they turn away, from Monotheism (التوحيد) or the message (الوصيّة). فَقُلْ آذَنْتُكُمْ ** then say, 'I have warned you all I have informed you of what I was commanded with. عَلى سَواءٍ ** on an equal footing. justly (عدل). وَإِنْ أَدْرِي وما ادري أَقَرِيبٌ أَمْ بَعِيدٌ ما تُوعَدُونَ ** And I do not know, and I am not aware: I do not know if what you are promised is near or far. but it is inevitable (كائن لا محال).

(١١٠) إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ ما تَكْتُمُونَ ** (110) Indeed He knows what is said openly, and He knows what you conceal. إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ ** (Indeed He knows what is said openly) refers to what you openly declare of slandering Islam وَيَعْلَمُ ما تَكْتُمُونَ ** (and He knows what you conceal) refers to the malice (الإحن) and grudges (الأحقاد) you harbor against the Muslims, and He will recompense you for it.

(١١١) وَإِنْ أَدْرِي لَعَلَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ لَكُمْ وَمَتاعٌ إِلى حِينٍ ** (111) I do not know, maybe it is a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while. وَإِنْ أَدْرِي لَعَلَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ لَكُمْ ** (I do not know, maybe it is a trial for you) meaning, I do not know; perhaps the delay of your recompense is a gradual enticement (استدراج) for you, and an increase in your temptation (افتتان), or a test (امتحان) to see how you act. وَمَتاعٌ إِلى حِينٍ ** (and an enjoyment for a while) meaning, an enjoyment until a predetermined term (أجل مقدّر) necessitated by His Will (مشيّته).

(١١٢) قالَ رَبِّ احْكُمْ بِالْحَقِ وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمنُ الْمُسْتَعانُ عَلى ما تَصِفُونَ ** (112) He said, 'My Lord! Judge with the truth. Our Lord is the All-beneficent, whose help is sought against what you allege.' قالَ رَبِّ احْكُمْ بِالْحَقِ. Al-Qummi said: Its meaning is: Do not leave the disbelievers, and the truth is the vengeance upon the wrongdoers (الظالمين). He said: And similar to it is in Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān: لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ ظالِمُونَ ** (You have no say in the matter, whether He shall relent toward them or punish them, for they are wrongdoers.) And it was recited (وقرء) as qāla (قال) in the narration of the saying of the Messenger (صَلّى الله عليه وآله وسلم): وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمنُ ** (Our Lord is the All-beneficent) meaning, abundant in mercy (كثير الرّحمة) toward His creation. الْمُسْتَعانُ ** (whose help is sought) meaning, the One from Whom assistance (المعونة) is requested. عَلى ما تَصِفُونَ ** (against what you allege) regarding the situation: that power (الشّوكة) will belong to them, and that the banner of Islam will flutter for a few days and then subside, and that if what was promised were true, it would have descended upon them. So, Allah answered the supplication (دعوة) of His Messenger, disappointed their hopes (امانيّهم), and granted victory to His Messenger over them. And it was recited with the tā’ (بالتاء).

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : من قرأ سورة الأنبياء حبّاً لها كان كمن رافق النّبييّن أجمعين في جنّات النعيم وكان مهيباً في أعين النّاس حياة الدّنيا. In Thawāb al-A‘māl and al-Majma‘, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Sūrat al-Anbiyā’ out of love for it, will be like one who accompanied all the Prophets (النّبييّن أجمعين) in the Gardens of Bliss (جنّات النعيم), and he will be held in awe (مهيباً) in the eyes of people during the life of this world (حياة الدّنيا)."
